He should’ve appeared earlier, before we even knew what would happen to Wallace. And even then, his character was meant to be comic relief but only made everything more cringe-worthy. This is a film I won’t be revisiting anytime soon, for very understandable reasons: a ridiculous story with no philosophical depth.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Uncut Gems
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
Is it strange to say that I had forgotten just how insane the final 40 minutes of this film are? Maybe. But at the same time, it feels natural, because I can’t remember any other film capable of making me feel this way - in such a dirty, relentless manner. Adam Sandler is so charismatic that you can’t help but root for his character until the very end, which makes everything more gripping and much harder to digest once it’s over.
Everything in this film is, in my view, executed extremely well, and the direction does almost all the magic - whether through the camerawork or the near-psychotic, stress-inducing editing. It’s a modern classic, and revisiting it only reinforces my stance on the film and further solidifies everything I have to say about this feature and about the Safdie brothers.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Under the Silver Lake
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
On a comedic note: this is basically "Beau Is Afraid", but instead of going to see his mother, he’s heading toward the girl — so they can maybe have sex in the future.

This film brings up some genuinely interesting points, especially regarding its core message: how is he more focused on finding the girl than uncovering all the twisted things going on in that city?

Extremely well-directed, with long takes that genuinely evoke classic noir films... I’m certain I’ll be rewatching this just to absorb more and connect all the dots.

By the way, the scene with the songwriter might be one of the boldest things I’ve ever seen in cinema — ever.

For now, I’m settling on 3.5 stars… I have a feeling it’s going to grow on me, little by little.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Under the Skin
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
What I took from this film: the plot isn’t what matters, it’s the perspective. 

What a sensational experience... So simple yet so abstract — visually haunting in a way that contrasts beautifully with the human experience, the experience of just being... human.
Scarlett’s character slowly begins to understand the meaning we, as humans, give to life — things like kindness, solidarity, care for others… or even monstrous instincts (like the man in the forest).
This was without a doubt a cinematic experience that will stay with me forever, in the best way possible. I also know this isn’t a film for everyone, and many might not enjoy the heavy abstraction… But me? I LOVED it — and I’m still considering the 5 stars.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Val
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
I tend to cry with this kind of documentary — and this one was no exception.
Even though it wasn’t Kilmer himself narrating (due to his throat cancer), it still felt incredibly intimate, and you could tell they made a real effort to keep that tone from beginning to end.

The ‘80s and ‘90s — an actor completely misunderstood, still untouched by the molds Hollywood tends to force onto artists like him, and with so much to say.
The use of personal footage and archives really helped paint a clearer picture of Val Kilmer’s reality and inner world, and I feel like the message came through perfectly:
“I’m more than my roles. I’m more than my voice.”

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: The Vanishing of Sidney Hall
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
I truly believe this could’ve been a brilliant film if it hadn’t included certain moments that felt, to me, emotionally manipulative and entirely unnecessary. I’m pretty sure most people watching had the same reaction I did after the opening scene: How the hell did a guy who talks like this become one of the most influential writers in the world?

That said, I don’t want to overshadow some of the genuinely well-executed parts. I think a lot of the discourse around Melody and her role in Sidney’s life is blown out of proportion. No, she’s not meant to be some ethereal “muse,” and no, she’s not annoying. She’s just a girl who falls in love with a boy—and vice versa. Their relationship was clearly built up, and I was genuinely invested in their future together as husband and wife.

Now, about those unnecessary moments: I’m mainly referring to the film’s final scenes between Sidney (Logan Lerman) and “The Searcher” (Kyle Chandler). They were incredibly dull and, frankly, a bit contradictory considering Sidney is supposed to be a genius. 
The so-called "plot twist"—if I can even call it that—completely missed the mark. It felt incredibly forced, even though it touched on sensitive topics like rape, domestic violence, and sexual harassment.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Warfare
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
Some people are really trying to twist the purpose of this film – I feel disoriented just reading some of the reviews.
This is not a propaganda piece, nor is it trying to label anyone as a hero or a villain. It's meant to portray the cowardice and dehumanization seen throughout the Iraq War – both in the actions of Iraqi forces and in the cruelty of many American soldiers.
This is an anti-war film, with a remarkably simple plot but filled with thought-provoking elements worth discussing. I just found it unnecessary to show images of the soldiers at the end, some with blurred or covered faces.

A film with a high level of difficulty in execution that still delivers – both in cinematography and in how it conveys pain, aggression, and fear through all its actors.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Waves
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
Please, always use protection.

On a more serious note... I'm still in shock from the sheer amount of emotions I felt throughout this project. And I honestly can't even express them properly — it was just that intense. This will stay with me forever, and I'm genuinely grateful to have come across something with so much meaning and so many important messages, especially in today’s world. I just hope more people get to watch it — it’s something special.

Ps: That scene in the garden... That moment between father and daughter, absolutely perfect.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: We Live in Time
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review: Why do I do this to myself? Deciding to go back and see something that left me feeling overwhelmed for days could never be a good plan.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: The Whale
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
As much as it made me cry my eyes out, I have to be critical of the project as a whole. It has quite a few flaws, especially in character development, and Thomas felt like he was thrown into the story at random. The critique (which, to me, is there, even if indirectly) of the U.S. healthcare system wasn’t well executed—but then again, I’m not sure if that was even their intention. It seemed like they wanted to address it but barely scratched the surface in a way that didn’t feel convincing.  

I can’t give it less than 3.5 stars because of the absolutely outstanding performances from every actor and the soundtrack throughout the film. They carried it on their backs.  

I also have to highlight the ending, which was clearly designed to make us cry. The symbolism and the analogy to the moment the father and daughter had at the beach were brilliant.

PS: When I die, I’d also like someone to read something for me… If I’m able, I might even recite my all-time favorite poem myself—"Guerra Civil" by Miguel Torga, a Portuguese author I highly recommend.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: When You Finish Saving the World
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
Well… what can I even say about this movie? To be honest, not much. It had a lot of potential — until I met the characters.  

There are positive aspects, the kind Jesse has consistently delivered in a decent, respectable way. But neither the mother nor the son grabbed me enough to call this a masterpiece. In fact, I’ll say it: I didn’t like any of the characters, and I feel like the ending wasn’t as satisfying because of that.  

I was genuinely expecting more… A guy who writes an absolutely flawless script for "A Real Pain" ends up choosing to write about a mother and son who are completely narcissistic? It didn’t land well for me.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: While We're Young
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review: Damn it... Now I wanna get high to the Blade Runner (1982) soundtrack too.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: The Witch
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
I know I'm a suspect, as you've noticed, I have a crush on Eggers: just like Caleb for his sister's breasts.

Pride is the worst sin. Glad I'm a sinner.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Woman Walks Ahead
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
I woke up wanting to watch a film... Just finished it and now I feel like going back to bed. It made me so sleepy I could honestly take a nap.

Definitely not the kind of project I enjoy the most — I'm even the kind of guy who says he doesn’t plan on watching many more like this for the rest of his life.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Woodshock
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
If I’d ever imagined Kirsten Dunst completely stoned, it would probably look something like this.

Started watching this with zero expectations and ended up with my jaw on the floor… Didn’t think it would be this interesting — or this visually stunning.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: X
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
Watching this just made me want to finish the "trilogy" as quickly as possible. The characters aren’t really introduced properly, and it feels intentional—setting things up for standalone films about Pearl and Maxine. 

Pearl, in particular, seems to have so much more to tell, and I’m genuinely excited to learn more about her. One detail I found absolutely brilliant was having Pearl and Maxine played by the same actress, which already hints at the idea that Pearl once had dreams similar to Maxine’s. In fact, you can pick up on that throughout the film, even if it’s not explicitly stated.  

The only reason I’m not giving this more than 4 stars is the horror aspect. I loved the psychological depth, and the horror elements were well-executed, but it lacked that *essential* touch—more unexpected, out-of-the-box moments.  

PS: The final scene shows the "program" Pearl and Howard were watching, and trust me, it’s worth sticking around for. It only made me more eager to learn about Maxine, especially with all the strict conservatism and religious fundamentalism surrounding her father.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Y2K
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review: Great. Now I can say I’ve heard someone saying "Oh my God, that’s Fred Durst". And believe me, that was the best line of the movie.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: You Hurt My Feelings
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
I’m on a long journey to watch all the A24 movies ever released. Today I visited their website and found this one on the A to Z list. It’s not my kind of movie, but it was still a decent way to pass the time.  

I also want to highlight the mutual suffering: a negative opinion makes me reflect on my ability to write poetry... But the goal is still to write a book.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: Zola
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
It was quite an interesting watch, especially knowing it was based on a true story. The actors themselves were brilliant, and I can’t stop thinking about the range Colman Domingo has—he can play SO many different roles, he’s a true genius in cinema, no doubt.
There are some issues though, especially with the script. Zola’s narration felt random—every 30 minutes or so we’d suddenly hear her voice telling the story, as if they forgot she was supposed to be narrating it all along. A few completely unnecessary scenes also took away from my overall experience, but all in all, a pretty solid film.

Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.name: The Zone of Interest
Every A24 Film That I Have Ever Seen.review:
A film that makes the air feel heavier with each passing moment, as the sound slowly takes over our senses.
The sound becomes so disturbingly overwhelming that all that remains in my mind are the atrocities committed beyond the mundane lives of these inhuman beings.
A camera work, in my view, out of the ordinary, with haunting images...
An ending that will stay in my mind forever.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Ikiru
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
Não consigo explicar o quão perfeito este filme é. A realidade é que estou neste momento a tentar arranjar palavras para o descrever.
Mais um clássico. E mais uma obra-prima. Mas este... Este entra fácil na minha lista de filmes preferidos. Por tudo o que representa, por todos os simbolismos e acima de tudo por uma conclusão simplesmente perfeita. Guião que me deixou completamente sem palavras. Em cada frase, em cada pausa, em cada expressão facial...
Toyo: "Depois de 30 anos sem faltar, mereces pelo menos 6 meses de folga". E que 6 meses intensos - aprender a viver novamente.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: The Lighthouse
Every Top 4 Entries.review: Moral of the story: don't EVER spill yer' beans.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Bo Burnham: Make Happy
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
It makes me cry every time I see it.
This project is so important to me…

Every Top 4 Entries.name: The Banshees of Inisherin
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
"He was a dull man, to be sure, but a dull man is better than a sharp man who takes off his own fingers."

This film will always have a special place in my heart. Forever. Rewatching this project gives me moments of inner reflection, moments where I think about my future, and it makes me feel alive in every single frame. Perfect.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Se7en
Every Top 4 Entries.review: The 30th birthday. In IMAX. One of my favourite films… always leaves me in shock.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Oppenheimer
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
One of the greatest films of all time.
Rewatching the most terrifying story humanity has ever experienced… It leaves me devastated every single time.
It’s a story that pushed me toward Physics, that made me follow this path — not because of the political context, but because of the names involved: from Rabi to Feynman, physicists who have always crossed my path, whether through mandatory study or my own personal research.
When I watched it for the first time, already knowing the whole story (thanks to my obsession with everyone involved), I was blown away by the level of detail. And the truth is, to this day, I’m still in awe of the dialogue and depth embedded in this project.

Man… I LOVE PHYSICS. Even when they called me stupid… and honestly, it’s exactly in those moments that I felt an even stronger urge to study harder.
Masochistic or not, it was a degree that brought me genuine joy with every passing year.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Sing Sing
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
A film about the corrupt justice system.
A film about the difficulty of being human.
A film about losing friends and family.
A film about the price of an act that, for many, might seem innocent.
A film about forgiveness.
But above all... a film about freedom, a film about humanity.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Sentimental Value
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
They called me a masochist for watching this film in the cinema twice within a week… they’ll probably call me the same again for having watched it a third time in such a short period of time, and honestly? I don’t care.

It continues, and will continue, to be impossible to describe the importance this project has in my life. It may sound strange, but the truth is that I will never forget the first time I watched this, alone, on the big screen. I’ll never forget the fear I felt when I walked into the theater with extremely high expectations, without even having watched a trailer or knowing what the film was about (on purpose, because of the impact and surprise it had on audiences).

What’s even more interesting is catching details that I missed the first times, like the monologue delivered by Elle Fanning and Stellan’s gaze, which says more than a thousand-page script ever could.

As I said in my previous reviews, it’s simply a perfect film, kept in a special corner of my heart for everything it represents, for everything it tells through dialogue and through distant, silent looks. A project that marked me for life.

“It's like her sadness is such an overwhelming part of her.”

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Cure
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
“You think there has to be a reason, a meaning behind the killings. But what if there isn’t one?”

I felt hypnotized the entire film — which only shows how powerful it is in every frame, every scene. A project that might sound cliché from its synopsis but becomes incredibly dark and philosophical as you slowly lose yourself in it.

A meticulous slow burn, with moments and lines that will stay with me forever — a masterpiece in every sense.
Every time I think about what was real or not, I get even more lost. I’ll probably keep replaying every possible moment in my head for days, maybe weeks. What a film.

Asian horror had already enchanted me with "Onibaba (1964)", but now it’s completely won me over — if that’s even possible in the truest sense of the word.

A perfect movie to watch on Halloween night.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Nosferatu
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
What a freaking masterpiece. Nothing more to say.
I've said it once and I'll say it again: Robert Eggers is my favorite filmmaker.

Also, the girl is more hornier than me.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: I'm Still Here
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
Now with my parents… And I'll keep watching, to never forget how it makes me feel. I'll show this to my sons/daughters... 

Even though I’m not Brazilian, I feel proud to have been born speaking this beautiful language.

The ice cream scene gets me every time.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Hereditary
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
This film is the culmination of everything I want in a horror film. Probably one of the best scripts I've ever seen on a project of this kind. So many symbolisms, so many precedents for everything that will happen, everything so well planned. We see it from Annie's perspective but we get to understand much more than she does and that was simply GENIUS. 
Chilling music, jumpscares that left my heart in my mouth, simply horrific and chilling content, it's truly something "a la Ari Aster"... You're crazy, I sincerely hope you never change, because we need more things like this.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Backrooms
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
And then… Renate Reinsve said “I am The Backrooms.”

This is probably going to be a completely different opinion from most people, and that’s perfectly fine, they’re the ones who are wrong, not me.

Horror is ALIVE, with so much still left to offer and entirely new subgenres continuing to emerge.

I want to start by saying that this rating doesn’t just represent what I felt while watching this on the big screen… it also exists to tell something to everyone who won’t find this film decent or substantial enough: you’re completely wrong. This is not an easy film to digest. As I said, it belongs to a completely different type of horror than anything we’ve seen before (and yes, we already survived Ari Aster). It isn’t slow, it simply wants to immerse us in that world, paying attention to detail and characters who clearly have much more left to say. Just feel it. Think about what you’re seeing and imagine everything the character must be thinking in that moment: the actors help enormously with that imaginative process.

I also need to thank Kane Parsons for not abandoning his original idea. It’s difficult to express how much admiration I have for people who hold onto their vision and try to show the best possible version of it. It’s even harder to take an idea that started small and project it onto the biggest screen possible. Kane, at 20 years old, managed to get two Oscar nominated actors working together. Kane, at 20 years old, managed to experiment with what a camera could or couldn’t do inside a space that feels simultaneously claustrophobic, familiar, and endless.

I also have to address the criticism I’ve been seeing in reviews, reels, and TikToks. This is not a conventional film. It’s not a film that places the entire story wide open right in front of you. People forget that a lot already existed before it reached cinemas. People forget this universe didn’t begin now, the story was already well established, structured, and explored. This film is only a small representation of everything that had already been created.

Renate Reinsve is simply perfect. And yes, I’m biased now, she’s my favorite actress. But believe me: she completely commands your attention from beginning to end, and her eyes say everything.

All that’s left for me to say is: give Parsons every possible budget imaginable so he can keep doing this. Not as a series (because that already happened on YouTube). I want something huge, just like this film.

Every Top 4 Entries.name: Marty Supreme
Every Top 4 Entries.review:
🏆 THE 98TH OSCARS PREPARATIONS 🏆

More than 60 minutes have passed since I finished watching this film. I got home and I’m now listening to the sound of rain falling while trying to calm myself down, if that’s even possible. Hardly.

I think everyone close to me knows that the Safdie Brothers have been climbing higher and higher in my personal ranking. 2025/2026 was no exception. Did I have extremely high expectations and was I scared because of that? Absolutely. My heart was racing and the film hadn’t even started yet. The moment I saw that very Safdie-esque transition at the beginning, I immediately realized the importance and the influence this project could have on the future of cinema - whether in editing, performances, cinematography, or even marketing campaigns. The ability Josh and the rest of the team (from producers to marketing directors) had to bring so many people into theaters to watch a film where the main character is obsessed with playing table tennis… I honestly have no words.

Give Chalamet the Oscar. What an actor, what a beast. He’s reached a level where he can completely erase every role he’s done or is currently doing from your memory and introduce a brand-new embodiment: one capable of putting us through stress, anxiety, and a complete inability to distinguish reality from fiction.

Once again, we find ourselves rooting for a narcissistic character who only cares about himself… where have I seen this before?

A modern classic. And my pick for Film of the Year 2025. I still haven’t seen Hamnet or Sentimental Value, but I honestly find it impossible for this opinion to change.

Favourites Of All Time.name: The Lighthouse
Favourites Of All Time.review: Moral of the story: don't EVER spill yer' beans.

Favourites Of All Time.review: The 30th birthday. In IMAX. One of my favourite films… always leaves me in shock.

Não consigo explicar o quão perfeito este filme é. A realidade é que estou neste momento a tentar arranjar palavras para o descrever.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Interstellar
Favourites Of All Time.review: I'm so scared of the future. The IMAX vibes just make everything worse.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Dead Poets Society
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Não queria ver algo novo, decidi ficar no aconchego de um filme preferido meu. 
Preferido porque tudo aquilo que me ensinaram sobre poesia, na escola, valeu zero para mim. E agora é uma das coisas mais importantes da minha vida. Aprendi sozinho, aprendi a entender versos à minha maneira e não à maneira dos outros.
Estamos aqui para viver, para quê ser realista? Prefiro sonhar e usar a poesia para enaltecer o facto de não ser uma máquina. Apenas um ser humano...

"Two roads diverged in a wood, and I—
I took the one less traveled by,
And that has made all the difference." - Robert Frost

Favourites Of All Time.name: The Banshees of Inisherin
Favourites Of All Time.review:
"He was a dull man, to be sure, but a dull man is better than a sharp man who takes off his own fingers."

This film will always have a special place in my heart. Forever. Rewatching this project gives me moments of inner reflection, moments where I think about my future, and it makes me feel alive in every single frame. Perfect.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Good Will Hunting
Favourites Of All Time.review: Fantastic film with the best company.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Bo Burnham: Make Happy
Favourites Of All Time.review:
It makes me cry every time I see it.
This project is so important to me…

Favourites Of All Time.name: Bo Burnham: Inside
Favourites Of All Time.review:
This is very  special to me. In every possible way. A safe place, a different world or some cozy spot to not be in panic.

Hope you’re doing fine Bo, we miss you…

Favourites Of All Time.name: Cure
Favourites Of All Time.review:
“You think there has to be a reason, a meaning behind the killings. But what if there isn’t one?”

I felt hypnotized the entire film — which only shows how powerful it is in every frame, every scene. A project that might sound cliché from its synopsis but becomes incredibly dark and philosophical as you slowly lose yourself in it.

A meticulous slow burn, with moments and lines that will stay with me forever — a masterpiece in every sense.

A perfect movie to watch on Halloween night.

Favourites Of All Time.name: La La Land
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Tive a oportunidade de rever este filme limitando pontos de escárnio ou sarcasmo. A realidade é que era demasiado puto quando vi pela primeira vez e decidi dar uma segunda oportunidade. Ainda bem que dei. É um filme quase perfeito. Um musical requintado, com letras incríveis, toques teatrais esplêndidos e interpretações emotivas, sensacionais.
É definitivamente um daqueles filmes que me vão ficar na memória. Porque quando era mais novo não tinha esta ligação com a cinematografia, não conseguia nem percebia o quão importante isto é para as pessoas, o poder de influenciar e fazer sentir coisas fora da caixa.
Ryan, Emma, que prazer de vos ver a encenar.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Soul
Favourites Of All Time.review:
“You can't crush a soul here, that's what life on Earth is for.” - 22

Sometimes we need to reconnect with our inner peace, especially in moments when stress, anxiety, and discouragement show up uninvited. I found this film during one of those moments, and I promised myself I would come back to it whenever I felt “lost” in things I still had to do: deadlines, responsibilities, times when I spend more time stuck in my room than outside, actually living life.

Don’t get me wrong, I love studying. I love working on projects that challenge me, that push me to my limits. But I reach a point where stopping feels like pressing a button covered in dust somewhere deep in my brain.

I used to be a lot like Joe… focused exclusively on what I could achieve academically and professionally. I forgot there was more to life than that.

This is a very special film to me. It reminds me of where I came from, what I’ve been through, and who I am now.

A project that… makes me stop. Think. Feel nostalgic for what I’ve lived and plan everything I still want to discover and experience.

Oh and by the way... Music is a big part of me, and without it... I would probably be one of those damned souls.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Hereditary
Favourites Of All Time.review:
This film is the culmination of everything I want in a horror film. Probably one of the best scripts I've ever seen on a project of this kind. We see it from Annie's perspective but we get to understand much more than she does and that was simply GENIUS. 
Chilling music, jumpscares that left my heart in my mouth, simply horrific and chilling content, it's truly something "a la Ari Aster"... You're crazy, I sincerely hope you never change, because we need more things like this.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Dune
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Preparing for the absolute PEAK.

I’m so excited, the best trilogy of all time? Debatable, but no one can deny the spectacular nature of these films. The landmark they represent.

Perfect.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Dune: Part Two
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Não consigo descrever o quão perfeito é este filme. Sim, eu já usei esta frase antes… E acreditem: tenho tentado não usar a palavra “perfeição” por ser forte demais para descrever algo. Mas aqui entramos noutro nível de cinematografia e de história. Edições lindas, passagens de tempo fenomenais, imagens de cortar a respiração, assuntos como fé, religião, esperança e guerra abordados de forma inimaginável… E a soundtrack… Posso soar, para aqueles que me conhecem, demasiado suspeito mas… Aquilo que o Zimmer fez com este filme tornou tudo ainda mais arrepiante. Parecia que quando entrava um som mais forte tudo à minha volta ficava ofuscado no sentido de me deixar ainda mais cativado, emerso na história.
A realidade é que esta obra é a real definição de cinema. Por tudo aquilo que representa, por tudo o que retrata… e por tudo o que foi feito atrás e à frente das câmaras.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Beautiful Boy
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Sem pestanejar. 5 estrelas. Foram capazes de usar um dos meus poemas preferidos de sempre. Sempre li ofegantemente, a pensar na minha vida e colocam assim, a meio e no final do filme, para impactar ainda mais. Nunca mais vou ser o mesmo. Bukowski escreveu um dos poemas mais simples, mais diretos - sem medos ou receios - tal como os pais deste miúdo, que viveram sem medo de enfrentar a situação. 
Chalamet, Carrell, sinto-me honrado... Ver-vos a representar estes papeis incomodou-me, deixou-me sem ar, fez-me chorar de forma insólita. Foi tudo tão bem planeado, tão expressivo, tão... especial.
Sampha na banda sonora e a última imagem... os dois, sentados em bancos de jardim... Levou-me ao pico da loucura, e ao pico do choro.

Favourites Of All Time.name: I'm Still Here
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Now with my parents… And I'll keep watching, to never forget how it makes me feel. I'll show this to my sons/daughters... 

Even though I’m not Brazilian, I feel proud to have been born speaking this beautiful language.

The ice cream scene gets me every time.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Fantastic Mr. Fox
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Ladies and gentlemen... Mr. Wes Anderson.
Nem sei o que dizer. Estou sem palavras, é tudo tão lindo, tão interativo, tão minucioso... Sentia-me uma criança, simplesmente encantado com cada adereço, cada animação, imagem, personagem, voz. 
Uma mensagem simples mas tão poderosa, um filme de animação que certamente os meus filhos vão ver.
É como vos disse: não sei o que dizer de tão incrível que é. Vejam.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Grave of the Fireflies
Favourites Of All Time.review:
I have few words to describe this project.
The theme may be quite “common” in cinema in general, but this movie managed to exceed all my expectations.

Perfect. That’s the word that defines everything I just watched.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Manchester by the Sea
Favourites Of All Time.review:
Isto é uma masterpiece ok? É o culminar do melhor que o drama pode dar: raiva, tristeza, choro, melancolia, pena. A história é tão simples mas tão cativante, tão dramática. 
Junta atores extraordinários, visuais simplistas mas credíveis, todas as imagens são importantes para o mood que querem incutir. 
Casey Affleck… És um monstro. Chorei, demasiado, e foste o maior culpado desse sucedido.

Favourites Of All Time.name: Memories of Murder
Favourites Of All Time.review:
I’m in complete shock.
Still thinking about the ending, still wondering if it was one person or another. Still processing the absurd number of suspects, all of them seemingly “normal.” I won’t go into the film’s specific message because I don’t want to spoil it — I don’t want to take away the pleasure of experiencing it for the first time, just as I did. Glued to the screen from the very first to the very last minute, ending with a final gasp and absolute shock. A masterpiece in every sense. A story that will stay with me forever.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: Hereditary
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
This film is the culmination of everything I want in a horror film. Probably one of the best scripts I've ever seen on a project of this kind. So many symbolisms, so many precedents for everything that will happen, everything so well planned. We see it from Annie's perspective but we get to understand much more than she does and that was simply GENIUS. 
Chilling music, jumpscares that left my heart in my mouth, simply horrific and chilling content, it's truly something "a la Ari Aster"... You're crazy, I sincerely hope you never change, because we need more things like this.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: Midsommar
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
Eu juro que estava indeciso entre 4 e 4 estrelas e meia. Passo a explicar: tinha e tenho medo de que as próximas vezes que vir este filme, não goste ainda mais do projeto. Tenho medo que esta masterpiece não cresça em mim de forma devida. 
Sim, disse masterpiece. Porque a realidade é que o Ari Aster foi capaz de transformar uma história "simples" em algo completamente macabro. Aquilo que mais me incomodava é que sempre conseguia perceber que alguma coisa se passava, mesmo no início do filme. Porque os atores e as próprias cenas foram tão minuciosamente colocadas que ficavas com a mera impressão de que algo iria acontecer. E acontecia, claro, é um filme de horror. Com poucos jumpscares (estava a contar), com simbolismos interessantíssimos, discursos perversos... E capaz de te mostrar o quão complicada pode ser a mente humana - complicada, bárbara, e por mais contraditório que seja, uma mente humana bastante desumana. 
Não dou 5 estrelas pelo que disse no início: quero voltar a ver isto, quero tentar perceber algumas cenas colocadas e acima de tudo quero entender a cabeça do Aster porque sinceramente... O gajo é um tolo varrido.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: Eddington
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
Watching it a second time made my rating change, and honestly, I wasn’t expecting that to happen at all. The final 40 minutes put any human being to the test - especially anyone aware of the sheer stupidity of what they’re witnessing. The fact that everything takes place in a single county, where the homeless man may be insane but the community can be even more so… it’s deeply satirical, and chilling to see a Sheriff portrayed this way. It’s almost unbelievable to experience this film from start to finish and take in the sheer number of extremes it represents.

Ari Aster, you already know everything I think about you and your films. But it never hurts to say it again: you’re one of my favorite directors, and that’s going to be very hard to change.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: C'est la Vie
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
Portanto, hoje não tive paciência para ver algo longo por isso decidi ir para as curtas metragens do Aster. Se aconselho? Ainda só vi dois, preciso de ver os restantes para ter uma opinião concreta. 
Sobre "C'est la vie", não há muito que referir, a sinopse diz tudo, o homem é insano e o final foi surpreendente para o pouco tempo que tem.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: The Turtle's Head
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
Bing, my friend, mine must be smaller, don't be sad.

On my journey to see everything by Ari Aster... I advise you to do the same, I just don't guarantee that you will end up mentally healthy

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: Munchausen
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
This guy has family problems… Seriously. 
The soundtrack was a bit loud… But I’ll say it again, I really like you Aster🫶

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: Herman’s Cure-All Tonic
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
I can only imagine Aster's therapist hearing and seeing this story for the first time.

A good criticism of the pharmaceutical industry.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: The Strange Thing About the Johnsons
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
This is f****d up man. This is too much for me to handle and I love weird projects. Not THIS weird. Like damn, that man was horny. It's a solid short film, but again... Damn.

Aster, my friend, if you want to talk about your family, just do it...

Ps: The actor who played the father also appears as the protagonist in the first "Beau" and I have to say, once again, that I still don't like his performances very much. Sorry.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: Basically
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
Pareceu mais um filme do Wes Anderson do que um do Ari Aster… 
Vou falar com o agente imobiliário do homem para ver se arranjo uma casa destas, sonho.

Ranking Ari Aster Films.name: Beau
Ranking Ari Aster Films.review:
Sobre este: é uma tentativa rasca de fazer algo absurdo em tão pouco tempo. Tudo bem que vi toda a grandiosidade do "Beau is Afraid" primeiro que isto... O Aster só mais tarde, depois destes minutos de Beau, decidiu já com dinheiro e tempo fazer algo mil vezes mais épico, mil vezes mais insano.
Não gostei muito deste ator, não me trouxe a complexidade da personagem que é Beau.

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: Sentimental Value
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
They called me a masochist for watching this film in the cinema twice within a week… they’ll probably call me the same again for having watched it a third time in such a short period of time, and honestly? I don’t care.

It continues, and will continue, to be impossible to describe the importance this project has in my life. It may sound strange, but the truth is that I will never forget the first time I watched this, alone, on the big screen. I’ll never forget the fear I felt when I walked into the theater with extremely high expectations, without even having watched a trailer or knowing what the film was about (on purpose, because of the impact and surprise it had on audiences).

What’s even more interesting is catching details that I missed the first times, like the monologue delivered by Elle Fanning and Stellan’s gaze, which says more than a thousand-page script ever could.

As I said in my previous reviews, it’s simply a perfect film, kept in a special corner of my heart for everything it represents, for everything it tells through dialogue and through distant, silent looks. A project that marked me for life.

“It's like her sadness is such an overwhelming part of her.”

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: Marty Supreme
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
🏆 THE 98TH OSCARS PREPARATIONS 🏆

More than 60 minutes have passed since I finished watching this film. I got home and I’m now listening to the sound of rain falling while trying to calm myself down, if that’s even possible. Hardly.

I think everyone close to me knows that the Safdie Brothers have been climbing higher and higher in my personal ranking. 2025/2026 was no exception. Did I have extremely high expectations and was I scared because of that? Absolutely. My heart was racing and the film hadn’t even started yet. The moment I saw that very Safdie-esque transition at the beginning, I immediately realized the importance and the influence this project could have on the future of cinema - whether in editing, performances, cinematography, or even marketing campaigns. The ability Josh and the rest of the team (from producers to marketing directors) had to bring so many people into theaters to watch a film where the main character is obsessed with playing table tennis… I honestly have no words.

Give Chalamet the Oscar. What an actor, what a beast. He’s reached a level where he can completely erase every role he’s done or is currently doing from your memory and introduce a brand-new embodiment: one capable of putting us through stress, anxiety, and a complete inability to distinguish reality from fiction.

Once again, we find ourselves rooting for a narcissistic character who only cares about himself… where have I seen this before?

A modern classic. And my pick for Film of the Year 2025. I still haven’t seen Hamnet or Sentimental Value, but I honestly find it impossible for this opinion to change.

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: One Battle After Another
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
It was a great idea to rewatch this film on the last day of the year. If I already thought it was incredible the first time I saw it in theaters, now… I don’t know, it feels like it grew on me even more, and I wasn’t expecting that at all. I genuinely thought my rating might go down for reasons that, looking back, feel simply stupid to even consider.

To me, this is a project with absolutely no flaws. Everything fits perfectly: everything is well executed, well detailed, and so, SO WELL directed. Perfect from start to finish. Funny and unsettling at the same time.

Even knowing exactly what was going to happen, the final chase still managed to surprise me again in terms of stress, emotion, and anxiety - PEAK cinema.

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: Sinners
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
Taking a little break from the A24 catalogue just to say that this is probably going to be the best 2025 film.
Hoping that Eddington is as good as this.

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: Bugonia
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
I think it’s completely understandable if people don’t consider this Yorgos’s best film - and, in my view, it doesn’t reach the madness of The Lobster or the visual and technical fascination of Poor Things. But let’s be honest: this was the most fun I’ve ever had with one of his films.

A movie capable of showing just how ridiculous a human being can be, where the project itself embraces that ridiculousness and pulls us into a universe that really isn’t that far from our own. It doesn’t take long for the insanity to escalate, but the last 30 minutes shine above everything that came before.

Yorgos… you always manage to surprise me, you know that.

Human beings have no solution. We are our own colonizers — one of the few species capable of destroying itself.

Ps: Jesse Plemons has been taking on roles I could easily see Dafoe playing… I kept watching and thinking: “Dafoe, is that you?”

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: Train Dreams
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
So: nominated for Best Actor at the Oscars, right?

I absolutely have to talk about Joel Edgerton, who delivered a staggering performance, hit with an emotional weight that’s impossible to ignore - watching him embody something like this gave me chills from beginning to end.

I struggled to settle on a rating for this project, because as visually stunning as it is, with a strong narrator and a compelling story, there are still a few things I would change - mainly in the middle section. It’s a feature that gets almost everything right, but by the end it left me interpreting the story in different ways and wishing certain scenes had been approached differently.
The way grief was portrayed was masterfully executed: it made me angry, it made me sad, it made me think about all the moments in my life where I felt like the main character… uncomfortable, melancholic - and absolutely beautiful.

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: Frankenstein
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
“Perhaps, I thought, this was the way of the world. It would hunt you and kill you just for being who you are”

I read a review that said this was the version where Viktor tried to be a father and somehow managed to be worse than usual - I think that perfectly sums up the film.

Guillermo del Toro has already spoiled us all with his meticulous attention to detail (especially in his recent projects), next-level production, and direction that keeps the viewer hooked from start to finish - but the truth is, this is the film that brings all of that together and showcases every quality that makes him such a brilliant director, writer, and producer.

Mia Goth takes on an extremely difficult role and delivers it flawlessly; Oscar Isaac is just Oscar Isaac (there’s no other way to describe him - genius), and Jacob Elordi transcends in this role, making the creature even more iconic, a bit more unsettling, and above all: much more human.

I just want everyone - Netflix included, and whoever decided to label this as “horror” - to understand that this project will never truly be considered a horror film.
Sure, Frankenstein is a horror story (though it has much more to it: ethical philosophy, morality, and a tragic love story), but this adaptation doesn’t belong to that concept of horror.

Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.name: The Secret Agent
Ranking The 98Th Oscars Best Picture Nom.review:
Tiago, forgive me, for I’m about to sin with this review.

I think it’s common knowledge by now how powerful and high-quality Brazilian cinema can be - the way it can move us, disturb us, and confront us, especially when dealing with dark, obscene, and painfully human subjects like this one. But I don’t think this film is for everyone, and honestly, it didn’t sit that well with me.

We’re talking about a story set during an extremely diabolical and ruthless dictatorship, where information is limited and everyone seems desperate to hide their past just to have a decent future. But when a film of this length tries to convey that idea in EVERY SINGLE SCENE, it becomes hard to stay engaged.

In the first few minutes, we already understand how hard life is for each character - they’re “refugees,” unable to build a proper life outside the city or even the country. We also quickly realize how little information existed at that time - and that’s enough to get the message across.

There were scenes so long they made me drift out of the film, and others where the camera would linger on a character meant for brief comic relief, staying there for what felt like endless seconds…

The story itself is well structured, but it bothered me not to see any real ending for the main character. Did it get the message about lack of information across? Yes - but I had already understood that moral two hours earlier.

Overall: an important message, a solid story, and an extremely well-executed production - but one that fails in editing and in truly communicating with its audience.

This is Brazil’s pick for the Oscars, so… let’s see how that turns out. 🇧🇷🎬

Oh, and by the way… that scene with the leg? Horrendous.

The Best Of A24.name: The Lighthouse
The Best Of A24.review: Moral of the story: don't EVER spill yer' beans.

The Best Of A24.name: Sing Sing
The Best Of A24.review:
A film about the corrupt justice system.
A film about the difficulty of being human.
A film about losing friends and family.
A film about the price of an act that, for many, might seem innocent.
A film about forgiveness.
But above all... a film about freedom, a film about humanity.

The Best Of A24.name: Aftersun
The Best Of A24.review:
Deixou-me despedaçado. Deixou-me parado, a olhar para o ecrã, no final do filme. Durante uns 10 minutos. Apenas a pensar em todos os detalhes que foram dando ao longo do filme sobre a depressão do Calum.
Um tópico tão importante e explicado de forma simplesmente… Brilhante. Não há outra palavra para definir, todos os momentos reais ou imaginados pela Sophie, todas as imagens e cinematografia, todos os diálogos, simples mas impactantes, todas as pequenas cenas que mostravam aquilo que o Calum era quando não estava com ela. Foi tudo tão bem representado que sou capaz de me lembrar de todos os pequenos momentos em que vias o quão o Calum não gostava de si mesmo… Cenas tão pequenas como ele cuspir a pasta de dentes para o espelho onde se via.

The Best Of A24.name: Everything Everywhere All at Once
The Best Of A24.review:
É um crime só ter visto isto agora. Ter desistido da Netflix e passado para a HBO Max começa a mostrar sinais de positivismo e bom presságio.
E como começar esta crítica? Eu nem sei o que dizer… Porque tudo o que vi foi tão bem conseguido, não encontro defeito algum, e ficava horas a escrever sobre tudo o que há de genial neste filme. Tenho a certeza absoluta que um outro “eu”, de outro universo, talvez uma árvore, talvez uma pedra, assistiu a este filme com o maior prazer possível. Everything… Perfeito. Everywhere… terei sempre a mesma opinião. All at once… com todas as emoções assoberbadas num único momento: colado à televisão a assistir um grande filme.

The Best Of A24.name: The Witch
The Best Of A24.review:
I know I'm a suspect, as you've noticed, I have a crush on Eggers: just like Caleb for his sister's breasts.

Pride is the worst sin. Glad I'm a sinner.

The Best Of A24.name: Hereditary
The Best Of A24.review:
This film is the culmination of everything I want in a horror film. Probably one of the best scripts I've ever seen on a project of this kind. So many symbolisms, so many precedents for everything that will happen, everything so well planned. We see it from Annie's perspective but we get to understand much more than she does and that was simply GENIUS. 
Chilling music, jumpscares that left my heart in my mouth, simply horrific and chilling content, it's truly something "a la Ari Aster"... You're crazy, I sincerely hope you never change, because we need more things like this.

The Best Of A24.name: C'mon C'mon
The Best Of A24.review:
This was beautiful. I can’t find another word to define what I just experienced… I might be biased when I call this film a masterpiece: I just became an uncle (well, a year and a few — I’d even say brief — months ago). What I saw here is everything I want to be for my nephew — his best friend, or at the very least, the person who annoys him the most.

Everything was perfect. The dialogues were beautiful. The rehearsals being read aloud as the film progressed revealed every single feeling — not just from the characters, but from all of us, both while watching and when waking up to start a new day.

I can’t keep writing or I’ll end up crying even more… Perfect.


"Jesse: [interviewer voice] Have you ever thought about the future?
[normal voice]
Jesse: Ahh yeah, whatever you plan on happening never happens. Stuff you would never think of happens. So you just have to c'mon c'mon, c'mon c'mon c'mon."

The Best Of A24.name: Good Time
The Best Of A24.review: "Beau is Afraid" but indie style, with less steroids, and in my opinion: much better.

The Best Of A24.name: Uncut Gems
The Best Of A24.review:
Is it strange to say that I had forgotten just how insane the final 40 minutes of this film are? Maybe. But at the same time, it feels natural, because I can’t remember any other film capable of making me feel this way - in such a dirty, relentless manner. Adam Sandler is so charismatic that you can’t help but root for his character until the very end, which makes everything more gripping and much harder to digest once it’s over.
Everything in this film is, in my view, executed extremely well, and the direction does almost all the magic - whether through the camerawork or the near-psychotic, stress-inducing editing. It’s a modern classic, and revisiting it only reinforces my stance on the film and further solidifies everything I have to say about this feature and about the Safdie brothers.

The Best Of A24.name: Backrooms
The Best Of A24.review:
And then… Renate Reinsve said “I am The Backrooms.”

This is probably going to be a completely different opinion from most people, and that’s perfectly fine, they’re the ones who are wrong, not me.

Horror is ALIVE, with so much still left to offer and entirely new subgenres continuing to emerge.

I want to start by saying that this rating doesn’t just represent what I felt while watching this on the big screen… it also exists to tell something to everyone who won’t find this film decent or substantial enough: you’re completely wrong. This is not an easy film to digest. As I said, it belongs to a completely different type of horror than anything we’ve seen before (and yes, we already survived Ari Aster). It isn’t slow, it simply wants to immerse us in that world, paying attention to detail and characters who clearly have much more left to say. Just feel it. Think about what you’re seeing and imagine everything the character must be thinking in that moment: the actors help enormously with that imaginative process.

I also need to thank Kane Parsons for not abandoning his original idea. It’s difficult to express how much admiration I have for people who hold onto their vision and try to show the best possible version of it. It’s even harder to take an idea that started small and project it onto the biggest screen possible. Kane, at 20 years old, managed to get two Oscar nominated actors working together. Kane, at 20 years old, managed to experiment with what a camera could or couldn’t do inside a space that feels simultaneously claustrophobic, familiar, and endless.

I also have to address the criticism I’ve been seeing in reviews, reels, and TikToks. This is not a conventional film. It’s not a film that places the entire story wide open right in front of you. People forget that a lot already existed before it reached cinemas. People forget this universe didn’t begin now, the story was already well established, structured, and explored. This film is only a small representation of everything that had already been created.

Renate Reinsve is simply perfect. And yes, I’m biased now, she’s my favorite actress. But believe me: she completely commands your attention from beginning to end, and her eyes say everything.

All that’s left for me to say is: give Parsons every possible budget imaginable so he can keep doing this. Not as a series (because that already happened on YouTube). I want something huge, just like this film.

The Best Of A24.name: Moonlight
The Best Of A24.review:
A revolutionary film in every sense: cinematography, performances, screenplay.
I'm trying to write something now so I can look back and remember what I felt watching this — but the truth is, nothing will ever make me forget this feature. Nothing.
Beautiful. Haunting. Dialogues that seem simple but carry so much emotional weight. Glances that will stay with me forever.
Such important topics, portrayed in three different phases of a miserable life... Sexuality, drugs, bullying — so much symbolic weight between scenes that it all built up to constant chills and tears.

Ps and edited: I now understand why La La Land didn't win an Oscar for best picture

The Best Of A24.name: Marty Supreme
The Best Of A24.review:
🏆 THE 98TH OSCARS PREPARATIONS 🏆

More than 60 minutes have passed since I finished watching this film. I got home and I’m now listening to the sound of rain falling while trying to calm myself down, if that’s even possible. Hardly.

I think everyone close to me knows that the Safdie Brothers have been climbing higher and higher in my personal ranking. 2025/2026 was no exception. Did I have extremely high expectations and was I scared because of that? Absolutely. My heart was racing and the film hadn’t even started yet. The moment I saw that very Safdie-esque transition at the beginning, I immediately realized the importance and the influence this project could have on the future of cinema - whether in editing, performances, cinematography, or even marketing campaigns. The ability Josh and the rest of the team (from producers to marketing directors) had to bring so many people into theaters to watch a film where the main character is obsessed with playing table tennis… I honestly have no words.

Give Chalamet the Oscar. What an actor, what a beast. He’s reached a level where he can completely erase every role he’s done or is currently doing from your memory and introduce a brand-new embodiment: one capable of putting us through stress, anxiety, and a complete inability to distinguish reality from fiction.

Once again, we find ourselves rooting for a narcissistic character who only cares about himself… where have I seen this before?

A modern classic. And my pick for Film of the Year 2025. I still haven’t seen Hamnet or Sentimental Value, but I honestly find it impossible for this opinion to change.

The Best Of A24.name: Climax
The Best Of A24.review:
Não vou mentir: dói-me não dar 5 estrelas. Tenho uma razão que me leva a crer que esta é a melhor pontuação que posso dar ao que assisti e acreditem, não é do filme, é de mim. 
É assustador ler pessoal a referir que este é o projeto menos "louco" do Noé... Pôs-me stressado, ansioso e desgastado psicologicamente. E a cena final  colocou-me num estado pior: degradado. 
Desde cinematografia, onde chegamos quase a perceber aquilo que o camera man está a sentir (coisa que nunca pensei dizer sobre um filme), passando por toda uma peça teatral (diria várias divididas em fases), indo para a capacidade que os atores tiveram para expressar a loucura e a sinistralidade da droga acabando no som. O som, do início ao fim é constrangedor, faz-te querer saltar da cadeira, subir uma parede e fugir só para não sentir a evolução da música. A própria forma como o som é projetado pela câmara, aqueles momentos em que o camera man seguia as personagens para fora do espaço "central", o tempo que foi acelerando à medida que tudo ficava ainda mais insano...
Acabo tentando realçar o porquê de não ter dado 5 estrelas: não sei se algum dia vou voltar a ver isto, tenho medo de voltar a sentir tudo o que senti.

The Best Of A24.name: Dream Scenario
The Best Of A24.review:
While watching this project, I kept thinking, "This is exactly the kind of movie Ari Aster would make, from the editing to the script itself"... When the credits started rolling, I read "Produced by Ari Aster," and my jaw dropped: one of the best plot twists ever. The man is haunting me... Or am I the one haunting him? Either way, great movie.

Edit: Another one from A24, let’s keep the momentum going!

The Best Of A24.name: Midsommar
The Best Of A24.review:
Eu juro que estava indeciso entre 4 e 4 estrelas e meia. Passo a explicar: tinha e tenho medo de que as próximas vezes que vir este filme, não goste ainda mais do projeto. Tenho medo que esta masterpiece não cresça em mim de forma devida. 
Sim, disse masterpiece. Porque a realidade é que o Ari Aster foi capaz de transformar uma história "simples" em algo completamente macabro. Aquilo que mais me incomodava é que sempre conseguia perceber que alguma coisa se passava, mesmo no início do filme. Porque os atores e as próprias cenas foram tão minuciosamente colocadas que ficavas com a mera impressão de que algo iria acontecer. E acontecia, claro, é um filme de horror. Com poucos jumpscares (estava a contar), com simbolismos interessantíssimos, discursos perversos... E capaz de te mostrar o quão complicada pode ser a mente humana - complicada, bárbara, e por mais contraditório que seja, uma mente humana bastante desumana. 
Não dou 5 estrelas pelo que disse no início: quero voltar a ver isto, quero tentar perceber algumas cenas colocadas e acima de tudo quero entender a cabeça do Aster porque sinceramente... O gajo é um tolo varrido.

The Best Of A24.name: A Ghost Story
The Best Of A24.review:
I’m still thinking about the rating I should give this project (I’m seriously considering 5 stars)… I’ve been trying to write something about what I just watched for 30 minutes, but the shock is so intense that I’m speechless. How can something so simple be so powerful… How did a Halloween costume manage to leave me in tears? I have a headache from crying so much.  
Suffering, loss — words that were very clearly expressed throughout the film… But "saudade" prevailed in my interpretation of it all. Absolutely beautiful.

The Best Of A24.name: The End of the Tour
The Best Of A24.review:
"He wants more than he has. I want precisely what he already has." – David Lipsky
I wasn’t prepared for this, and honestly, I never thought a project like this would affect me the way it did. I really expected it to be dull or at least boring at times, but I was hooked as soon as I started to understand more about the personalities of the two characters.
What a journey. It really makes you reflect on what you want for your future, and I nearly had an existential crisis when I heard these lines:
"David thought books existed to stop you from feeling lonely. If I could, I'd say to David that living those days with him reminded me of what life is like, instead of being a relief from it. And I'd tell him it made me feel much less alone."
A simple yet effective cinematography — this is a film that places all its weight on the script and the actors, and they were absolutely breathtaking.

The Best Of A24.name: Pearl
The Best Of A24.review:
It’s impressive how every family dinner scene in A24 horror films are, as a great colleague and friend—Mr. Martin Scorsese—would say, "Absolute Cinema." The dinner between the father (though barely present), mother, and daughter was simply brilliant—fear, panic, anxiety, stress, anguish—all culminating in a single scene.  

Mia Goth’s monologue will stay with me forever. I can’t stop thinking about everything she said and the way she delivered it.

I was completely mesmerized the entire time, unable to take my eyes off every little detail I could catch. I found it truly extraordinary how subtly they transported me to that era—through the clothing, the atmosphere, and the portrayal of the hopes (or dreams) of that time. Simply amazing.

And the ending—almost "comedic"—reminded me of "Looney Tunes". Just her unhinged, psychotic expression alone managed to leave me both unsettled and laughing at the same time.

I’m still considering the 5 stars—for now, I’ll leave it as is. If something shifts in me, I’ll add a note to this review.

The Best Of A24.name: Under the Skin
The Best Of A24.review:
What I took from this film: the plot isn’t what matters, it’s the perspective. 

What a sensational experience... So simple yet so abstract — visually haunting in a way that contrasts beautifully with the human experience, the experience of just being... human.
Scarlett’s character slowly begins to understand the meaning we, as humans, give to life — things like kindness, solidarity, care for others… or even monstrous instincts (like the man in the forest).
This was without a doubt a cinematic experience that will stay with me forever, in the best way possible. I also know this isn’t a film for everyone, and many might not enjoy the heavy abstraction… But me? I LOVED it — and I’m still considering the 5 stars.

The Best Of A24.name: The Lobster
The Best Of A24.review:
Yorgos, és um maníaco, insanamente marado, louco... Deixas-me sempre sem palavras quando acabo de ver um filme teu.
Tanto simbolismo, tanta metáfora e coisas tão hiperbolizadas mas que fazem TANTO sentido dentro da sociedade atual... Um espelho completamente exagerado do nosso mundo, deixou-me tão desconfortável e tão pensativo ao mesmo tempo - genial.
Cinematografia arrepiante, diálogos super estranhos, às vezes quase que nem parece bem representado pelo ator em si... E no fim chegas à conclusão que era tudo de propósito para pensares fora da caixa, para te deixar fora da tua zona de conforto.

The Best Of A24.name: A Different Man
The Best Of A24.review:
I started watching this film without even reading the synopsis... I had no idea what it was about, nor what genre it belonged to—and I honestly think that was the best decision I could’ve made.

Sadness, anger, stress, anxiety, joy, and pure fun—all concentrated in a deeply philosophical project. It ends where it all began, and that moment was the peak of everything I felt throughout the film.

A comedy with hints of psychological thriller, and I never thought I’d find a mix like this... I had so much fun, but at the same time, the stress kept building up until it burst in all directions the moment Oswald said: “Oh my friend, you haven’t changed a bit.”

Sebastian... Adam... You were both so incredible—I struggle to put into words just how powerful your performances were. Watching you was an absolute privilege.

The Best Of A24.name: The Drama
The Best Of A24.review:
Kristoffer Borgli, you keep surprising me and climbing higher and higher in my personal ranking.
I genuinely don’t understand some of the negative opinions… sure, everyone has their own taste, but you can’t walk into a cinema expecting a rom-com just because the story revolves around a couple. Especially when we’re talking about Borgli, an extraordinary mind, delivering sharp social commentary in his films, chaotic editing that somehow holds together an avalanche of emotions, and camera work that keeps getting more refined.
Borgli expands his eccentricity almost like a funnel, but in reverse: he starts with something intimate and calm, builds a connection with the characters, and then gradually increases the suspense, the tension, and those awkward moments until you reach a point where you don’t even know if you should laugh, cry, or just look away from the screen because of how uncomfortable everything feels. And that’s exactly why I wanted to see this in a cinema: until now, I hadn’t had the chance to experience one of his films on the big screen.
A24 really has some brilliant minds in their hands, people capable of creating projects that stay with you for a long time. And this one even had Ari Aster as a producer (I swear, those two must love working together).

The Best Of A24.name: Civil War
The Best Of A24.review:
Complicado descrever o que senti ao ver isto. Vou mais além e digo que é mesmo impossível. Houveram cenas em que me apetecia saltar da cadeira de tão arrepiado que me sentia. E não falo em específico, refiro-me sim ao geral do filme. 
Frases e diálogos extremamente bem conseguidos, com duplos significados e de compreensão profunda.
Mas o que mais me impressionou foi mesmo o rumo que deram: não é um filme sobre guerra, é um filme sobre jornalismo de guerra. Tudo o que sofrem, toda a adrenalina que sentem no momento… Simplesmente TUDO, bem representado por cada personagem apresentada.
Não minto que dava um final diferente à história, com toques mais satíricos e hiperbólicos, mas overall, muito bem conseguido.
Sobre esta crítica? Não consigo descrever melhor… “Yeah, that'll do.”

The Best Of A24.name: We Live in Time
The Best Of A24.review: Why do I do this to myself? Deciding to go back and see something that left me feeling overwhelmed for days could never be a good plan.

The Best Of A24.name: Swiss Army Man
The Best Of A24.review:
This was so chaotic and fun... It honestly felt strange watching the man who defeated Voldemort fart his way through an hour and a half.  
But the truth is, beneath all the chaos and weirdness, the moral of the story hits surprisingly hard: the ending even made me shed a tear — I wasn’t expecting it to leave me so lost in thought.

Honestly, I'll never forget most of this movie, hilarious, funny and extremely introspective.

PS: I loved the holy bible joke.

The Best Of A24.name: Past Lives
The Best Of A24.review:
Não sei porquê mas mesmo com paragens absurdas no HiMovies consegui ficar completamente agarrado à história. Geralmente quando não consigo ver em condições um filme fico completamente desconectado e acabo por só ignorar e tentar ver outro. Este foi diferente. Personagens muito bem construídas, soundtrack absurda. Cinematografia bastante aceitável e convidativa.
E é só doloroso acabar assim. Dá vontade de partir algo à minha volta, mas não me apetecia muito limpar o estrago depois. Fico no desespero mental e no sufoco que me proporcionou.
Acho que vou só ficar agarrado ao som daquele piano quando os dois se despedem e aceitar que a vida nem sempre corre como desejamos: uns alcançam sonhos de criança, outros tentam ser felizes com pouco.

The Best Of A24.name: The Zone of Interest
The Best Of A24.review:
A film that makes the air feel heavier with each passing moment, as the sound slowly takes over our senses.
The sound becomes so disturbingly overwhelming that all that remains in my mind are the atrocities committed beyond the mundane lives of these inhuman beings.
A camera work, in my view, out of the ordinary, with haunting images...
An ending that will stay in my mind forever.

The Best Of A24.name: The Farewell
The Best Of A24.review:
What a breath of fresh air, what a beautiful film, what a wonderful story... I completely fell in love with the family — I wanted to know everything, every interaction and relationship, from beginning to end.
A screenplay that gives you chills throughout, paired with a simple yet effective cinematography — I couldn’t ask for much more from a film like this.
'Based on an actual lie' — a line that will stay with me.

The Best Of A24.name: Causeway
The Best Of A24.review:
I don't care what anyone else thinks about this… I've read so many comments saying it's boring — enough. This is hauntingly beautiful and chilling. The pacing was nearly perfect, I was completely drawn to the characters, I wanted to know more about the two of them, and I honestly don't think it was just because of the actors: the script is chilling, simple, and effective.

The ending left me in pieces, and the final line perfectly captured what both of them were looking for: “I'm trying to make a friend.” Sometimes, just a quiet cup of coffee in the morning with the right company can mean the world.

The Best Of A24.name: Eighth Grade
The Best Of A24.review:
I can’t quite put into words what this movie made me feel, but I’ll try, I promise... I connected deeply with Kayla — the struggle of navigating puberty, especially when everything around you feels like a fog, uncertain and unexplored.  

The car scene left me completely uncomfortable, and I can’t explain the anger I felt watching it all unfold "right in front of me." The conversation between Kayla and her dad was truly special, showing not only her struggle with being a teenager but also his "unknowingness" in being — well, a dad.  

It was a moment where I genuinely felt understood. We’re all a bit strange in our own way, and I know I wasn’t easy to handle when I was younger. Bo, you never stop surprising me — you’re simply brilliant, and it’s a joy to watch everything you create. Thank you for showing such a complex and sometimes "dark" side of this age in such a powerful, honest way.

The Best Of A24.name: Waves
The Best Of A24.review:
Please, always use protection.

On a more serious note... I'm still in shock from the sheer amount of emotions I felt throughout this project. And I honestly can't even express them properly — it was just that intense. This will stay with me forever, and I'm genuinely grateful to have come across something with so much meaning and so many important messages, especially in today’s world. I just hope more people get to watch it — it’s something special.

Ps: That scene in the garden... That moment between father and daughter, absolutely perfect.

The Best Of A24.name: First Reformed
The Best Of A24.review:
Exactly 27 minutes have passed since I finished watching this film…
So far, I’ve only stared at my computer screen with Letterboxd open. I don’t think I’ve even blinked, and I can feel my body frozen, covered in goosebumps.

It’s a film with such emotional, cultural, ethical, religious, and philosophical weight that I’m certain I’ll be thinking about it for weeks—maybe even months—as I recommend it to friends and family.
Every criticism aimed at the Church is delivered in a way that made my heart skip a beat whenever something out of the ordinary happened... The same goes for every time Toller read from his journal: my heart would race, my nerves would spike, and the build-up of tension exploded in that final act.

An ending that left me speechless—so spiritual, so profound, with touches of redemption. Almost imaginary... or was it real?

The Best Of A24.name: Minari
The Best Of A24.review:
I couldn’t have watched this at a better time.
An emotionally demanding film: the story seems so simple, yet every scene and every image carries deep meaning.
The ending completes the cycle—mirroring the planting season shown earlier—absolutely brilliant.
The actors turn the whole thing into a beautiful piece of art, and the soundtrack sends shivers from start to finish.

The Best Of A24.name: After Yang
The Best Of A24.review:
I know the theme represented in this project has been done before, but the way the story was told made everything feel special.

The cinematography is out of this world — absolutely fascinating and unafraid to explore different types of imagery and camera work. The use of color was incredibly detailed, paired with a script that had me hooked from beginning to end.

The scenes where Jake "visited" Yang’s memories left me breathless, they were just so beautiful. The conversation between the android and Kyra left me speechless, and the short dialogue between Ada and Jake in the car completely floored me — so simple, yet so powerful.

An incredible experience, and I’m sure I’ll want to rewatch this film again someday.

The Best Of A24.name: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
The Best Of A24.review:
It was an incredible experience, and yes, everyone loves Marcel.
The peculiarities of this film were so well used, so well structured, so well executed. I was honestly surprised at how deeply I felt connected to the little guy.
Even the “problems” tied to fame, or the difficulty of adjusting to a new environment, far from family — I teared up when Marcel started talking about it.

I really wasn’t expecting it… what a breath of fresh air — something so different and so good, so heartwarming.

Top 10 Animated Movies.name: Soul
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
“You can't crush a soul here, that's what life on Earth is for.” - 22

Sometimes we need to reconnect with our inner peace, especially in moments when stress, anxiety, and discouragement show up uninvited. I found this film during one of those moments, and I promised myself I would come back to it whenever I felt “lost” in things I still had to do: deadlines, responsibilities, times when I spend more time stuck in my room than outside, actually living life.

Don’t get me wrong, I love studying. I love working on projects that challenge me, that push me to my limits. But I reach a point where stopping feels like pressing a button covered in dust somewhere deep in my brain.

I used to be a lot like Joe… focused exclusively on what I could achieve academically and professionally. I forgot there was more to life than that.

This is a very special film to me. It reminds me of where I came from, what I’ve been through, and who I am now.

A project that… makes me stop. Think. Feel nostalgic for what I’ve lived and plan everything I still want to discover and experience.

Oh and by the way... Music is a big part of me, and without it... I would probably be one of those damned souls.

Top 10 Animated Movies.name: Grave of the Fireflies
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
I have few words to describe this project.
The theme may be quite “common” in cinema in general, but this movie managed to exceed all my expectations.

Perfect. That’s the word that defines everything I just watched.

Top 10 Animated Movies.name: Fantastic Mr. Fox
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
Ladies and gentlemen... Mr. Wes Anderson.
Nem sei o que dizer. Estou sem palavras, é tudo tão lindo, tão interativo, tão minucioso... Sentia-me uma criança, simplesmente encantado com cada adereço, cada animação, imagem, personagem, voz. 
Uma mensagem simples mas tão poderosa, um filme de animação que certamente os meus filhos vão ver.
É como vos disse: não sei o que dizer de tão incrível que é. Vejam.

Top 10 Animated Movies.name: Flow
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
Yes, I have to give 5 stars. Not only because it's beautiful but also to say: suck it, Disney and Dreamworks.
So touching and at the same time so simple... Sadness, anger and joy culminated in a piece of art. Beautiful.

Top 10 Animated Movies.name: Look Back
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
Everyone has an artistic side that can be expressed in countless ways. Writing is part of my routine - I write for myself, but sometimes I think about the possibility of someone reading my poetry and feeling connected to me. Writing belongs to me, but reading also shares connections and emotions; reading also makes us human.

Anyone even slightly interested in manga knows how inhumane the industry can be... stories of artists who admit to sleeping only two or three hours a day just to release a new chapter. The love for art surpasses any kind of hard work - and it’s easy to see that in our own lives: when students love a subject, they’re far more likely to succeed in it than in one they dislike but force themselves through just to keep up. Working on something you love always carries an immense weight in your success.

A story that gives you chills even before you realize it’s semi-autobiographical. Something that will stay with me forever. 

No more words - perfect.

Top 10 Animated Movies.name: Porco Rosso
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
"I'd rather be a pig than a fascist"

The best Miyazaki film I've seen so far, and without a doubt one of Studio Ghibli's finest. What a wonderful film to experience - not only for its animation and story, but for the entire philosophy behind the pig and the post–First World War setting.
What impressed me the most was how simply the main character is introduced: few words, no over-explaining, and yet an entire life and a quiet tragedy are immediately felt. The "curse" of his pig form is presented with such restraint that it becomes even more powerful - a symbol that speaks for itself without ever needing to be spelled out.
It's fun, genuinely funny, with BRILLIANT touches of classic romanticism in the main character, and it carries the best message I could ever ask for in a film: FASCISM NEVER AGAIN, or, in Portuguese, FASCISMO NUNCA MAIS.

Top 10 Animated Movies.name: A Charlie Brown Christmas
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
Charlie Brown: "I think there must be something wrong with me, Linus. Christmas is coming, but I'm not happy. I don't feel the way I'm supposed to feel."

This short film is truly beautiful. It’s been far too long since I last watched it - I only vaguely remembered a few scenes, and yet it feels special in every possible way. A Christmas tree can be the most beautiful one we've ever seen, but if it isn't made with care, attention, and affection, it ends up feeling almost empty.
The way Charlie is portrayed is striking, because of everything he represents: depression, fear of how others will react, and the belief that he's worth nothing… It's such an important message, especially for this time of year.
