Top 10 Animated Movies.name: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
Top 10 Animated Movies.review:
It was an incredible experience, and yes, everyone loves Marcel.
The peculiarities of this film were so well used, so well structured, so well executed. I was honestly surprised at how deeply I felt connected to the little guy.
Even the “problems” tied to fame, or the difficulty of adjusting to a new environment, far from family — I teared up when Marcel started talking about it.

I really wasn’t expecting it… what a breath of fresh air — something so different and so good, so heartwarming.

Top 10 Short Films.name: The Tell-Tale Heart
Top 10 Short Films.review:
Yes. Another one with a 5-star rating. And yes... I'm biased in this review, it's one of my all-time favorite stories, and one of my favorite directors.

Before watching a film by Eggers, whether it's a short or a feature, I always keep in mind that the atmosphere experience will truly be important to the progression of the story. The reality is that all the settings and the old man's way of being were extremely unsettling.

The very expression of the servant, the contradiction between the love and hatred he had for the old man, was so well conveyed that it left me completely chilled and afraid to watch until the end, knowing and being very familiar with the story.

Everything in this short film reminded me of one of the most terrifying phrases I’ve ever read in a tale, especially coming from Poe:
“Now this is the point. You fancy me a mad. Madmen know nothing. But you should have seen me. You should have seen how wisely I proceeded...”

Top 10 Short Films.name: If Anything Happens I Love You
Top 10 Short Films.review: I can't express what I felt... Poetry and suffering, together, in movement.

Top 10 Short Films.name: Retirement Plan
Top 10 Short Films.review: 🏆 THE 98TH OSCARS PREPARATIONS 🏆 "I will be present. So aggressively present." Wow… We forget so easily that this is what we live for. A day filled with so much worry, whether at work or, in my case, at university, where stress and anxiety take center stage and end up driving all our decisions. And it all starts to feel a bit pointless… pointless in the sense that one problem can easily turn out to be the least of our problems, if that makes sense. We’re here to live, not to suffer. I have a similar list, but with even more items on it. I just hope I’ll have the time to do everything I’ve imagined doing, seeing, or experiencing, whether during retirement or in the limited vacations I’ll have in my professional future. This animated short film made me feel something I’ve never felt with films from this genre before, which is why I gave it this rating. How can seven minutes be so introspective? How can such a simple list make me feel "saudade" for things I haven’t even done yet?

Top 10 Short Films.name: Pieta
Top 10 Short Films.review:
“I sometimes have memories of things that could never have happened. Maybe they belong to somebody else.”

I’m genuinely not okay… was this really Joachim Trier’s first project? What an absolute banger. So much suffering, such radical emotional shifts, such a raw depiction of loss…

Joachim would later revisit these themes throughout his career, and honestly, I know very few directors capable of expressing this level of emotion in stories like these. I was going to say that this short film explores the deepest sides of loneliness, suicide, aging, and memory, but the truth is that all of Trier’s projects approach these subjects in such a raw and beautiful way…

Top 10 Short Films.name: The clown within us
Top 10 Short Films.review:
I promote this film with all my heart, just as Eduardo gave his own in making this short film. A Portuguese film, in true Portuguese fashion, with beautiful images of a city I’m honored to know and be part of, our beautiful Porto. The color, the camera, the actor, the acts, and the music — all brought together in an 18-minute session, everything perfect, with the love for his father always present.

Now in Portuguese: que maravilha Eduardo, a saudade e o amor descritos de forma tão simples mas tão especial, tão comovente, espero que vás longe❤️

Top 10 Short Films.name: Vincent
Top 10 Short Films.review:
An absolute masterpiece. I’m speechless. How can something so short have such an impact?

Oh and by the way… Same, Vincent — Edgar Allan Poe drives me crazy too, but I love him for it.

Top 10 Short Films.name: Former Cult Member Hears Music for the First Time
Top 10 Short Films.review:
I think I went through the same thing when I heard Geese for the first time.
Did I hit my head on a table? No, but I did feel like screaming from all the different sounds I was hearing (Cameron Winter, you magnificent beast).

Jokes aside... an EXTREMELY IMPORTANT message.

Top 10 Short Films.name: Butterfly
Top 10 Short Films.review:
🏆 THE 98TH OSCARS PREPARATIONS 🏆

I don’t think I’ve ever been this shocked by transitions in my life. The way moments of life flowed into scenes was so beautifully executed that I found myself staring in awe from start to finish. A life story told through an artistic lens, paying tribute to difficult times in sports.

The butterfly stroke is my favorite, and I’m sure that every time I swim, I’ll think of this film. At least for a while… Swimming makes me forget things, but it also brings back memories, moments that were meaningful in my life, or simply striking to my own perspective. This project firmly belongs to the latter.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Ikiru
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Não consigo explicar o quão perfeito este filme é. A realidade é que estou neste momento a tentar arranjar palavras para o descrever.
Mais um clássico. E mais uma obra-prima. Mas este... Este entra fácil na minha lista de filmes preferidos. Por tudo o que representa, por todos os simbolismos e acima de tudo por uma conclusão simplesmente perfeita. Guião que me deixou completamente sem palavras. Em cada frase, em cada pausa, em cada expressão facial...
Toyo: "Depois de 30 anos sem faltar, mereces pelo menos 6 meses de folga". E que 6 meses intensos - aprender a viver novamente.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: The Banshees of Inisherin
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
"He was a dull man, to be sure, but a dull man is better than a sharp man who takes off his own fingers."

This film will always have a special place in my heart. Forever. Rewatching this project gives me moments of inner reflection, moments where I think about my future, and it makes me feel alive in every single frame. Perfect.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Beautiful Boy
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Sem pestanejar. 5 estrelas. Foram capazes de usar um dos meus poemas preferidos de sempre. Sempre li ofegantemente, a pensar na minha vida e colocam assim, a meio e no final do filme, para impactar ainda mais. Nunca mais vou ser o mesmo. Bukowski escreveu um dos poemas mais simples, mais diretos - sem medos ou receios - tal como os pais deste miúdo, que viveram sem medo de enfrentar a situação. 
Chalamet, Carrell, sinto-me honrado... Ver-vos a representar estes papeis incomodou-me, deixou-me sem ar, fez-me chorar de forma insólita. Foi tudo tão bem planeado, tão expressivo, tão... especial.
Sampha na banda sonora e a última imagem... os dois, sentados em bancos de jardim... Levou-me ao pico da loucura, e ao pico do choro.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Dune: Part Two
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Não consigo descrever o quão perfeito é este filme. Sim, eu já usei esta frase antes… E acreditem: tenho tentado não usar a palavra “perfeição” por ser forte demais para descrever algo. Mas aqui entramos noutro nível de cinematografia e de história. Edições lindas, passagens de tempo fenomenais, imagens de cortar a respiração, assuntos como fé, religião, esperança e guerra abordados de forma inimaginável… E a soundtrack… Posso soar, para aqueles que me conhecem, demasiado suspeito mas… Aquilo que o Zimmer fez com este filme tornou tudo ainda mais arrepiante. Parecia que quando entrava um som mais forte tudo à minha volta ficava ofuscado no sentido de me deixar ainda mais cativado, emerso na história.
A realidade é que esta obra é a real definição de cinema. Por tudo aquilo que representa, por tudo o que retrata… e por tudo o que foi feito atrás e à frente das câmaras.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Nosferatu
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
What a freaking masterpiece. Nothing more to say.
I've said it once and I'll say it again: Robert Eggers is my favorite filmmaker.

Also, the girl is more hornier than me.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Aftersun
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Deixou-me despedaçado. Deixou-me parado, a olhar para o ecrã, no final do filme. Durante uns 10 minutos. Apenas a pensar em todos os detalhes que foram dando ao longo do filme sobre a depressão do Calum.
Um tópico tão importante e explicado de forma simplesmente… Brilhante. Não há outra palavra para definir, todos os momentos reais ou imaginados pela Sophie, todas as imagens e cinematografia, todos os diálogos, simples mas impactantes, todas as pequenas cenas que mostravam aquilo que o Calum era quando não estava com ela. Foi tudo tão bem representado que sou capaz de me lembrar de todos os pequenos momentos em que vias o quão o Calum não gostava de si mesmo… Cenas tão pequenas como ele cuspir a pasta de dentes para o espelho onde se via.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Manchester by the Sea
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Isto é uma masterpiece ok? É o culminar do melhor que o drama pode dar: raiva, tristeza, choro, melancolia, pena. A história é tão simples mas tão cativante, tão dramática. 
Junta atores extraordinários, visuais simplistas mas credíveis, todas as imagens são importantes para o mood que querem incutir. 
Casey Affleck… És um monstro. Chorei, demasiado, e foste o maior culpado desse sucedido.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Fantastic Mr. Fox
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Ladies and gentlemen... Mr. Wes Anderson.
Nem sei o que dizer. Estou sem palavras, é tudo tão lindo, tão interativo, tão minucioso... Sentia-me uma criança, simplesmente encantado com cada adereço, cada animação, imagem, personagem, voz. 
Uma mensagem simples mas tão poderosa, um filme de animação que certamente os meus filhos vão ver.
É como vos disse: não sei o que dizer de tão incrível que é. Vejam.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: James Acaster: Repertoire
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Provavelmente as melhores sessões de stand up que alguma vez vi. Digo isto sendo um fanático por Ricky Gervais e humor negro. 
Este rapaz nasceu para a comédia, nasceu para contar histórias. É impressionante a forma como ele se expressa, às vezes parece quase improvisado e só torna tudo mil vezes melhor. O que mais me espantou foi o facto de ele ter uma espécie de história principal e contar histórias intermédias no meio da anterior. Todas as punch lines que ele coloca nas intermédias são depois usadas no final da história principal - um génio na escrita, uma moral simplesmente sensacional. 
O próprio uso do corpo, o uso dos POUQUÍSSIMOS objetos que possui no palco e principalmente o uso das palavras, O USO DAS PALAVRAS. Porque tudo o que ele diz tem um propósito - faz te rir no momento em que ele te conta… e faz te chorar de rir nas consequências que ele apresenta, após o uso “indevidamente” devido de algumas palavras.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Everything Everywhere All at Once
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
É um crime só ter visto isto agora. Ter desistido da Netflix e passado para a HBO Max começa a mostrar sinais de positivismo e bom presságio.
E como começar esta crítica? Eu nem sei o que dizer… Porque tudo o que vi foi tão bem conseguido, não encontro defeito algum, e ficava horas a escrever sobre tudo o que há de genial neste filme. Tenho a certeza absoluta que um outro “eu”, de outro universo, talvez uma árvore, talvez uma pedra, assistiu a este filme com o maior prazer possível. Everything… Perfeito. Everywhere… terei sempre a mesma opinião. All at once… com todas as emoções assoberbadas num único momento: colado à televisão a assistir um grande filme.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: I Lost My Body
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Só me apeteceu escrever esta review agora, no dia seguinte. E ainda estou sem saber o que dizer. 
Fico incomodado com a média das pontuações aqui no Letterboxd, parece que não viram o mesmo filme que eu. A melancolia, o aperto no coração, a tristeza e o stress apareceram de tal maneira que é-me impossível definir, ou redigir algo sobre o processo de recuperação. É estranho dizer isto sobre um filme, mas marcou-me tanto que preciso realmente de recuperar emocionalmente. 
Não vou falar sobre o filme em si, entraria em spoilers desnecessários. E acho que ainda não estou preparado para poder sequer falar sobre ele.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Perfect Days
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Há muita coisa a acontecer na minha vida. Demasiado caótica, sinto que pode ser uma simples vida tornada em transtornos momentâneos. 

Precisava de paz, de acalmar os ânimos, de perceber a beleza de cada situação que aparecia no meu dia. Foram 2 horas em que tudo à minha volta desapareceu. Todos os meus problemas ausentaram-se e só tenho a agradecer por isso. Um plot tão simples mas tão imersivo, fez-me acalmar, viver o momento. Quiseram mostrar o quão os momentos mais simples da vida podem ser os mais bonitos - e hoje vou apanhar ar, vou lá fora olhar para as árvores, olhar para o céu. 

"KOMOREBI: is the Japanese word for the shimmering of light and shadows that is created by leaves swaying in the wind. It only exists once, AT THAT MOMENT."

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Conclave
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Estou de queixo caído.
Estou sem escrever uma review para isto há horas porque sempre que adicionava palavras só me apetecia apagar tudo.
Só vou mesmo agradecer por não ter lido (ainda) o livro porque o final deixou-me, como podem ver, sem saber o que dizer. 
Imagens lindíssimas, história com muito tea, tal como eu adoro.
Ralph Fiennes, um privilégio ver-te a representar um papel tão complexo. Foste simplesmente brilhante.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Perfeito. A palavra que define este filme. Pôs-me a chorar de alegria, pôs-me a chorar de tristeza. 
Cheguei rapidamente à conclusão de que não conseguiria apagar a memória de alguém assim, desta maneira. Tudo bem que não tenho essa opção, como o Joel teve, mas pensar em eliminar todas as memórias que tenho com uma pessoa, já custa imenso... apagá-las totalmente, seria um inferno.
Que história lindíssima. 
Repito: perfeito.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Climax
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Não vou mentir: dói-me não dar 5 estrelas. Tenho uma razão que me leva a crer que esta é a melhor pontuação que posso dar ao que assisti e acreditem, não é do filme, é de mim. 
É assustador ler pessoal a referir que este é o projeto menos "louco" do Noé... Pôs-me stressado, ansioso e desgastado psicologicamente. E a cena final  colocou-me num estado pior: degradado. 
Desde cinematografia, onde chegamos quase a perceber aquilo que o camera man está a sentir (coisa que nunca pensei dizer sobre um filme), passando por toda uma peça teatral (diria várias divididas em fases), indo para a capacidade que os atores tiveram para expressar a loucura e a sinistralidade da droga acabando no som. O som, do início ao fim é constrangedor, faz-te querer saltar da cadeira, subir uma parede e fugir só para não sentir a evolução da música. A própria forma como o som é projetado pela câmara, aqueles momentos em que o camera man seguia as personagens para fora do espaço "central", o tempo que foi acelerando à medida que tudo ficava ainda mais insano...
Acabo tentando realçar o porquê de não ter dado 5 estrelas: não sei se algum dia vou voltar a ver isto, tenho medo de voltar a sentir tudo o que senti.

Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.name: Onibaba
Top 15 First Time Watches In 2024.review:
Ando a tentar explorar filmes de terror/horror quase centenários, ou pelo menos, mais antigos que os meus pais (não vos chamei de velhos, amo-vos) e chego à conclusão que foi o melhor que podia fazer: estou realmente farto destes novos filmes de terror, já tinha abordado isso na review do Nosferatu (1922). 
A cena da noite com a tempestade a surgir... Acho difícil não considerar como uma das melhores cenas da história da cinematografia. Máscara simplesmente horripilante, o medo instalava-se na minha cabeça assim que anoitecia, tudo incrível.
"The fear of sexual liberation".

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Cure
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
“You think there has to be a reason, a meaning behind the killings. But what if there isn’t one?”

I felt hypnotized the entire film — which only shows how powerful it is in every frame, every scene. A project that might sound cliché from its synopsis but becomes incredibly dark and philosophical as you slowly lose yourself in it.

A meticulous slow burn, with moments and lines that will stay with me forever — a masterpiece in every sense.
Every time I think about what was real or not, I get even more lost. I’ll probably keep replaying every possible moment in my head for days, maybe weeks. What a film.

Asian horror had already enchanted me with "Onibaba (1964)", but now it’s completely won me over — if that’s even possible in the truest sense of the word.

A perfect movie to watch on Halloween night.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Good Time
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review: "Beau is Afraid" but indie style, with less steroids, and in my opinion: much better.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Sing Sing
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
A film about the corrupt justice system.
A film about the difficulty of being human.
A film about losing friends and family.
A film about the price of an act that, for many, might seem innocent.
A film about forgiveness.
But above all... a film about freedom, a film about humanity.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Memories of Murder
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
I’m in complete shock.
Still thinking about the ending, still wondering if it was one person or another. Still processing the absurd number of suspects, all of them seemingly “normal.” I won’t go into the film’s specific message because I don’t want to spoil it — I don’t want to take away the pleasure of experiencing it for the first time, just as I did. Glued to the screen from the very first to the very last minute, ending with a final gasp and absolute shock. A masterpiece in every sense. A story that will stay with me forever.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: The Witch
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
I know I'm a suspect, as you've noticed, I have a crush on Eggers: just like Caleb for his sister's breasts.

Pride is the worst sin. Glad I'm a sinner.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: I'm Still Here
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Now with my parents… And I'll keep watching, to never forget how it makes me feel. I'll show this to my sons/daughters... 

Even though I’m not Brazilian, I feel proud to have been born speaking this beautiful language.

The ice cream scene gets me every time.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: C'mon C'mon
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
This was beautiful. I can’t find another word to define what I just experienced… I might be biased when I call this film a masterpiece: I just became an uncle (well, a year and a few — I’d even say brief — months ago). What I saw here is everything I want to be for my nephew — his best friend, or at the very least, the person who annoys him the most.

Everything was perfect. The dialogues were beautiful. The rehearsals being read aloud as the film progressed revealed every single feeling — not just from the characters, but from all of us, both while watching and when waking up to start a new day.

I can’t keep writing or I’ll end up crying even more… Perfect.


"Jesse: [interviewer voice] Have you ever thought about the future?
[normal voice]
Jesse: Ahh yeah, whatever you plan on happening never happens. Stuff you would never think of happens. So you just have to c'mon c'mon, c'mon c'mon c'mon."

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Uncut Gems
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Is it strange to say that I had forgotten just how insane the final 40 minutes of this film are? Maybe. But at the same time, it feels natural, because I can’t remember any other film capable of making me feel this way - in such a dirty, relentless manner. Adam Sandler is so charismatic that you can’t help but root for his character until the very end, which makes everything more gripping and much harder to digest once it’s over.
Everything in this film is, in my view, executed extremely well, and the direction does almost all the magic - whether through the camerawork or the near-psychotic, stress-inducing editing. It’s a modern classic, and revisiting it only reinforces my stance on the film and further solidifies everything I have to say about this feature and about the Safdie brothers.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Nosferatu
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
What a freaking masterpiece. Nothing more to say.
I've said it once and I'll say it again: Robert Eggers is my favorite filmmaker.

Also, the girl is more hornier than me.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: The Northman
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
"You must choose between kindness for your kin, and hatred for your enemies."

Robert Eggers is officially my new favourite director. What a freaking masterpiece. Not prepared for the Hamlet analogy, still in shock to be honest.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Grave of the Fireflies
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
I have few words to describe this project.
The theme may be quite “common” in cinema in general, but this movie managed to exceed all my expectations.

Perfect. That’s the word that defines everything I just watched.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: One Battle After Another
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
It was a great idea to rewatch this film on the last day of the year. If I already thought it was incredible the first time I saw it in theaters, now… I don’t know, it feels like it grew on me even more, and I wasn’t expecting that at all. I genuinely thought my rating might go down for reasons that, looking back, feel simply stupid to even consider.

To me, this is a project with absolutely no flaws. Everything fits perfectly: everything is well executed, well detailed, and so, SO WELL directed. Perfect from start to finish. Funny and unsettling at the same time.

Even knowing exactly what was going to happen, the final chase still managed to surprise me again in terms of stress, emotion, and anxiety - PEAK cinema.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Look Back
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Everyone has an artistic side that can be expressed in countless ways. Writing is part of my routine - I write for myself, but sometimes I think about the possibility of someone reading my poetry and feeling connected to me. Writing belongs to me, but reading also shares connections and emotions; reading also makes us human.

Anyone even slightly interested in manga knows how inhumane the industry can be... stories of artists who admit to sleeping only two or three hours a day just to release a new chapter. The love for art surpasses any kind of hard work - and it’s easy to see that in our own lives: when students love a subject, they’re far more likely to succeed in it than in one they dislike but force themselves through just to keep up. Working on something you love always carries an immense weight in your success.

A story that gives you chills even before you realize it’s semi-autobiographical. Something that will stay with me forever. 

No more words - perfect.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Moonlight
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
A revolutionary film in every sense: cinematography, performances, screenplay.
I'm trying to write something now so I can look back and remember what I felt watching this — but the truth is, nothing will ever make me forget this feature. Nothing.
Beautiful. Haunting. Dialogues that seem simple but carry so much emotional weight. Glances that will stay with me forever.
Such important topics, portrayed in three different phases of a miserable life... Sexuality, drugs, bullying — so much symbolic weight between scenes that it all built up to constant chills and tears.

Ps and edited: I now understand why La La Land didn't win an Oscar for best picture

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Flow
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Yes, I have to give 5 stars. Not only because it's beautiful but also to say: suck it, Disney and Dreamworks.
So touching and at the same time so simple... Sadness, anger and joy culminated in a piece of art. Beautiful.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Dream Scenario
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
While watching this project, I kept thinking, "This is exactly the kind of movie Ari Aster would make, from the editing to the script itself"... When the credits started rolling, I read "Produced by Ari Aster," and my jaw dropped: one of the best plot twists ever. The man is haunting me... Or am I the one haunting him? Either way, great movie.

Edit: Another one from A24, let’s keep the momentum going!

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Bugonia
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
I think it’s completely understandable if people don’t consider this Yorgos’s best film - and, in my view, it doesn’t reach the madness of The Lobster or the visual and technical fascination of Poor Things. But let’s be honest: this was the most fun I’ve ever had with one of his films.

A movie capable of showing just how ridiculous a human being can be, where the project itself embraces that ridiculousness and pulls us into a universe that really isn’t that far from our own. It doesn’t take long for the insanity to escalate, but the last 30 minutes shine above everything that came before.

Yorgos… you always manage to surprise me, you know that.

Human beings have no solution. We are our own colonizers — one of the few species capable of destroying itself.

Ps: Jesse Plemons has been taking on roles I could easily see Dafoe playing… I kept watching and thinking: “Dafoe, is that you?”

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: A Ghost Story
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
I’m still thinking about the rating I should give this project (I’m seriously considering 5 stars)… I’ve been trying to write something about what I just watched for 30 minutes, but the shock is so intense that I’m speechless. How can something so simple be so powerful… How did a Halloween costume manage to leave me in tears? I have a headache from crying so much.  
Suffering, loss — words that were very clearly expressed throughout the film… But "saudade" prevailed in my interpretation of it all. Absolutely beautiful.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Sinners
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Taking a little break from the A24 catalogue just to say that this is probably going to be the best 2025 film.
Hoping that Eddington is as good as this.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Eddington
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Watching it a second time made my rating change, and honestly, I wasn’t expecting that to happen at all. The final 40 minutes put any human being to the test - especially anyone aware of the sheer stupidity of what they’re witnessing. The fact that everything takes place in a single county, where the homeless man may be insane but the community can be even more so… it’s deeply satirical, and chilling to see a Sheriff portrayed this way. It’s almost unbelievable to experience this film from start to finish and take in the sheer number of extremes it represents.

Ari Aster, you already know everything I think about you and your films. But it never hurts to say it again: you’re one of my favorite directors, and that’s going to be very hard to change.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: First Reformed
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Exactly 27 minutes have passed since I finished watching this film…
So far, I’ve only stared at my computer screen with Letterboxd open. I don’t think I’ve even blinked, and I can feel my body frozen, covered in goosebumps.

It’s a film with such emotional, cultural, ethical, religious, and philosophical weight that I’m certain I’ll be thinking about it for weeks—maybe even months—as I recommend it to friends and family.
Every criticism aimed at the Church is delivered in a way that made my heart skip a beat whenever something out of the ordinary happened... The same goes for every time Toller read from his journal: my heart would race, my nerves would spike, and the build-up of tension exploded in that final act.

An ending that left me speechless—so spiritual, so profound, with touches of redemption. Almost imaginary... or was it real?

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: The Zone of Interest
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
A film that makes the air feel heavier with each passing moment, as the sound slowly takes over our senses.
The sound becomes so disturbingly overwhelming that all that remains in my mind are the atrocities committed beyond the mundane lives of these inhuman beings.
A camera work, in my view, out of the ordinary, with haunting images...
An ending that will stay in my mind forever.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Under the Skin
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
What I took from this film: the plot isn’t what matters, it’s the perspective. 

What a sensational experience... So simple yet so abstract — visually haunting in a way that contrasts beautifully with the human experience, the experience of just being... human.
Scarlett’s character slowly begins to understand the meaning we, as humans, give to life — things like kindness, solidarity, care for others… or even monstrous instincts (like the man in the forest).
This was without a doubt a cinematic experience that will stay with me forever, in the best way possible. I also know this isn’t a film for everyone, and many might not enjoy the heavy abstraction… But me? I LOVED it — and I’m still considering the 5 stars.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Pearl
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
It’s impressive how every family dinner scene in A24 horror films are, as a great colleague and friend—Mr. Martin Scorsese—would say, "Absolute Cinema." The dinner between the father (though barely present), mother, and daughter was simply brilliant—fear, panic, anxiety, stress, anguish—all culminating in a single scene.  

Mia Goth’s monologue will stay with me forever. I can’t stop thinking about everything she said and the way she delivered it.

I was completely mesmerized the entire time, unable to take my eyes off every little detail I could catch. I found it truly extraordinary how subtly they transported me to that era—through the clothing, the atmosphere, and the portrayal of the hopes (or dreams) of that time. Simply amazing.

And the ending—almost "comedic"—reminded me of "Looney Tunes". Just her unhinged, psychotic expression alone managed to leave me both unsettled and laughing at the same time.

I’m still considering the 5 stars—for now, I’ll leave it as is. If something shifts in me, I’ll add a note to this review.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: A Different Man
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
I started watching this film without even reading the synopsis... I had no idea what it was about, nor what genre it belonged to—and I honestly think that was the best decision I could’ve made.

Sadness, anger, stress, anxiety, joy, and pure fun—all concentrated in a deeply philosophical project. It ends where it all began, and that moment was the peak of everything I felt throughout the film.

A comedy with hints of psychological thriller, and I never thought I’d find a mix like this... I had so much fun, but at the same time, the stress kept building up until it burst in all directions the moment Oswald said: “Oh my friend, you haven’t changed a bit.”

Sebastian... Adam... You were both so incredible—I struggle to put into words just how powerful your performances were. Watching you was an absolute privilege.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Mickey 17
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
This is the culmination of a sleepless night, an empty bottle of Vodka, a heated discussion about the state of the world and a comedy special starring myself, Diogo. 

What's more fun than this? Only my grade for the last subject I need to complete my degree.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: The Metamorphosis of Birds
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
“A distância daqueles que nós amamos é tão difícil. Porém ela é também muito construtiva. Constrói quem eu sou.”

Poetry and cinema joined forces to create this film.
During this short and chaotic journey of mine through cinema, there has always been something that bothered me: not knowing and not having truly felt Portuguese film and Portuguese cinema. I’m here to change that, and I feel like I started in the best way possible - what an unbelievable project.
Unsettling dialogues, meaningful from start to finish, with metaphors that follow us as we get to know the family, all culminating in that final scene. Chilling from beginning to end, a poem that came straight from the heart.

I feel an enormous pride in being Portuguese and in seeing my culture represented in such a beautiful way. A pride in our Latin roots, a pride in our mixture, a pride in the Portuguese saudade of being who we are.

“Deixa-me sempre muito espantado como é que uma coisa tão infinita consegue entrar por nós a dentro” - e agora em português, o oceano é assustador, mas será sempre a nossa casa.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Causeway
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
I don't care what anyone else thinks about this… I've read so many comments saying it's boring — enough. This is hauntingly beautiful and chilling. The pacing was nearly perfect, I was completely drawn to the characters, I wanted to know more about the two of them, and I honestly don't think it was just because of the actors: the script is chilling, simple, and effective.

The ending left me in pieces, and the final line perfectly captured what both of them were looking for: “I'm trying to make a friend.” Sometimes, just a quiet cup of coffee in the morning with the right company can mean the world.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: Waves
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
Please, always use protection.

On a more serious note... I'm still in shock from the sheer amount of emotions I felt throughout this project. And I honestly can't even express them properly — it was just that intense. This will stay with me forever, and I'm genuinely grateful to have come across something with so much meaning and so many important messages, especially in today’s world. I just hope more people get to watch it — it’s something special.

Ps: That scene in the garden... That moment between father and daughter, absolutely perfect.

Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.name: The End of the Tour
Top 30 First Time Watches In 2025.review:
"He wants more than he has. I want precisely what he already has." – David Lipsky
I wasn’t prepared for this, and honestly, I never thought a project like this would affect me the way it did. I really expected it to be dull or at least boring at times, but I was hooked as soon as I started to understand more about the personalities of the two characters.
What a journey. It really makes you reflect on what you want for your future, and I nearly had an existential crisis when I heard these lines:
"David thought books existed to stop you from feeling lonely. If I could, I'd say to David that living those days with him reminded me of what life is like, instead of being a relief from it. And I'd tell him it made me feel much less alone."
A simple yet effective cinematography — this is a film that places all its weight on the script and the actors, and they were absolutely breathtaking.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: The Front Room
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
Me, after a day of pure chaos (I have a test next monday):
"I'm going to see an incredible film... The directors are Robert Eggers' brothers so it can't go wrong." 

Well... This is really bad.

Trying to combine comedy with horror is a very difficult job, and I don't remember a project like this that went well. 
"Scream" turns out to be quite comical...

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Emilia Pérez
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
I can only imagine the director: "Well, my friends, let's take taboo subjects of the 21st century and not research anything about them... Yes, because I am knowledgeable and I don't need to know anything else for this to come to fruition in a dignified way. Oh, and now we're going to make a film with Mexican characters, why not hire actors who don't know how to speak Spanish? What a great idea.”
I think I could speak better Spanish than Selena Gomez. I want to be famous, hire me.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Revenge of the Green Dragons
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
What a waste of time.
Editing? I’ve seen PowerPoint transitions with more finesse.
A plot straight out of the "How to Be Clichê" handbook, violence for the sake of something, actors who seemed just as confused as I was, and a TERRIBLE cinematography
Honestly, I’m impressed — it takes effort to miss every mark this consistently.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: This Is the End
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
Well… this was bad. Really bad.
I didn’t laugh once, and I was this close to turning it off and just aimlessly scrolling through YouTube until I fell asleep. Watching this was definitely not a good plan.

By the way, don’t ever make me hear or read “Michael Cera is dead” again — I wouldn’t be able to handle it. Don’t hurt my GOAT🐐

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: A Glimpse Inside the Mind of Charles Swan III
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
The best way to express how much I hated this movie is through the rating I’m about to give it… I feel like I wasted an hour and a half, though I’m at least grateful it was a shorter film.  

Labeled as a comedy, yet I laughed a grand total of zero times.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Lamb
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
I love weird movies… but I prefer the kind of projects that make me think—strange yet philosophical. The premise they gave this feature film is just plain stupid, and there’s no real sense to it.  

I was on the verge of giving it half a star, I swear. But I won’t deny that there are some stunning scenes, breathtaking visuals—if not for that, I wouldn’t have finished the movie. And speaking of the ending… what was that? I get the idea that others had done something similar in the past, but her portrayal, combined with the camera focusing on her expressions, didn’t convince me at all.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Dicks: The Musical
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
A musical meant to make you laugh… Unfortunately, I felt so much cringe that the jokes ended up fading into the background. I tried to give it a chance—there are a few (very few) positives, with one or two solid songs, but other than that...  

The best part of it was Megan Thee Stallion.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: False Positive
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
Discomfort the entire time—and honestly, it’s excellent to feel something like that in a project like this. I felt extremely anxious, stressed, unsure of what to think, and they implemented that feeling really well. But…  

The final act might be one of the worst I’ve ever seen in terms of cinematography—some scenes were downright terrible and extremely bizarre. I spent an hour waiting for a strong conclusion, only to end up wishing I hadn’t seen any of it. It felt like even the actors weren’t trying anymore, as if they had given up on the script long ago.  

A horror movie I have no intention of revisiting, with extremely obvious and unconvincing jumpscares. The suspense saved a lot of scenes, and because of that, I have to give it a higher rating than I originally planned.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Mojave
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
I can’t believe I had the courage to watch this all the way through. Not even Oscar Isaac could save it.
Such mediocre and unrelatable characters — it was honestly cringe-worthy every time they randomly threw in Shakespeare and Stuart Mill into conversations, it never felt natural.
And the ending... they might as well have killed each other, I would’ve been much happier with that — what a misery.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Slice
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
A24… you keep surprising me, but now in the worst way possible. I never thought watching Chance The Rapper playing a werewolf would be something I checked off my 2025 list — and honestly, I wish I hadn’t.
The cinematography was actually pretty decent, but the acting fell flat and the script was too bad to be true: the witches were the absolute peak of mediocrity. I laughed a grand total of zero times and still managed to call every single character cringe — now that’s a great comedy! (See, Austin? I managed to be ironic and satirical better than you, and in way less time.)
Anyway, I’ll keep going with this A24 journey… I just hope I don’t come across more films like this. I still believe A24 is the best studio out there.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Dark Places
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
I read a comment that said this movie was shot similarly to The Office... Well, let me just say The Office has better cinematography than this.

I walked into this film fully aware it was going to be bad. And yet... I still left even more disappointed. It’s a rare feeling, but when it hits? Damn, it hurts.

The performances were so weak they felt robotic... I feel bad for Hoult, he was the only one who actually pulled it off here.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Tusk
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review: From the moment I saw Lily-Rose Depp acting, I told myself, “Well, this just got interesting.” The same can't be said for Johnny Depp—by the time he showed up, I’d already seen too much. He should’ve appeared earlier, before we even knew what would happen to Wallace. And even then, his character was meant to be comic relief but only made everything more cringe-worthy. This is a film I won’t be revisiting anytime soon, for very understandable reasons: a ridiculous story with no philosophical depth.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Woman Walks Ahead
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
I woke up wanting to watch a film... Just finished it and now I feel like going back to bed. It made me so sleepy I could honestly take a nap.

Definitely not the kind of project I enjoy the most — I'm even the kind of guy who says he doesn’t plan on watching many more like this for the rest of his life.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Outlaws
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
Honestly? I'm still trying to figure out how I didn’t switch it off halfway through.
It’s already the kind of genre I run from, but somehow they seasoned it with just the right mix of cringe and chaos to make it borderline unwatchable.
Terrible performances, cinematography flatter than a pancake, and a storyline so predictable I could’ve written it in my sleep — or worse, had ChatGPT do it.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: The Monster
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
This was so creatively bankrupt it made me rethink my entire ambition of becoming a world-famous director. You know that delusion — "I’ll make a horror film so unforgettable it’ll haunt people forever"? Yeah, that died a slow, boring death about 15 minutes into this.
It explains nothing about "the monster", the characters are painfully predictable, and the plot is ridiculously simple and bland.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: The Sky Is Everywhere
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
Another project I honestly don’t know how I made it through… The message might be important and, in some ways, well delivered — but the story itself was so dull and lacking in substance.
The cinematography was all over the place, with so many different elements thrown in that I got tired of every scene pretty quickly.

Yes, it has some interesting points, and sure, the cinematography wasn’t all bad the entire time…
But I just can’t give it a higher rating than this.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Into the Forest
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
What even happened here? Why did they lose power in the first place? And sorry to whoever wrote this story, but the dad’s death was completely unnecessary and just plain stupid. One more thing: why did they burn down the house? Makes zero sense.

And look, the points I just mentioned don’t even begin to cover the suffering I went through during the runtime... Mistake after mistake, a script with more holes than my grandpa’s hairline.
The only thing that slightly saved this project were the performances and the halfway decent cinematography — because other than that, it’s just not worth it.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: The Adderall Diaries
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
I just couldn’t take this film seriously.
Not sure if it was Amber Heard or the story itself — maybe both — but it really didn’t work for me.
Way too many unnecessary scenes (especially around Stephen and Lana’s relationship), painfully basic cinematography, and a plot so predictable I felt like I could’ve written it in my sleep.
It honestly felt like they threw in a bunch of last-minute ideas hoping it would make things cooler… but nope, it just made everything worse.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: Son of a Gun
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
Predictable, dull, and flavorless — that’s all I can really take from this film.
Was I expecting much? No.
Did I think it would be this bland? Also no.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: The Ballad of Lefty Brown
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
Yes, I’ve said it plenty of times: I don’t like westerns.
But imagine this — I’d rather win a bet and watch the entire A24 catalogue than give up halfway through because of this film. 
There are definitely some positives, mostly in the cinematography: beautiful shots, many of them simple, evoking the classic essence of the genre.
It’s a film that tries to cast heroism onto a character who was never built to be a hero.
Many scenes were just plain dull (speaking as someone who has no plans to revisit this type of story).

The actors were strong, sure, but even they couldn’t save it from being a slog.

Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.name: The Hole in the Ground
Worst First Time Watches Of 2025.review:
A film with no substance, no explanations, and characters making completely irrational decisions (never in my life would I go running or exercising in the forest knowing what's out there and that things clearly aren’t safe).
I feel like I have a lot more to say about how the film was shot than anything else, because honestly, the colors and cinematography really impressed me — that’s a major positive.
But it fails in pretty much everything else.

Alex Garland.name: Alex Garland
Alex Garland.bio:
Alexander Medawar Garland (born 26 May 1970) is an English author, screenwriter, and director. He rose to prominence with his novel The Beach (1996). He received praise for writing the Danny Boyle films 28 Days Later (2002) and Sunshine (2007), as well as Never Let Me Go (2010) and Dredd (2012). In video games, he co-wrote Enslaved: Odyssey to the West (2010) and was a story supervisor on DmC: Devil May Cry (2013).

Garland made his directorial debut when he wrote and directed the sci-fi thriller Ex Machina (2014). He earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Original Screenplay. He won three British Independent Film Awards, including Best Screenplay, Best Director, and Best British Independent Film for the film. His second movie, Annihilation (2018), an adaptation of the 2014 novel of the same name, was a critical success. He wrote, directed, and executive produced the FX miniseries Devs (2020), followed by the horror thriller Men (2022) and the dystopian action thriller Civil War (2024). He also co-directed the war film Warfare (2025). A24 produced the three films.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Alex Garland, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

films.name: Civil War
films.review:
Complicado descrever o que senti ao ver isto. Vou mais além e digo que é mesmo impossível. Houveram cenas em que me apetecia saltar da cadeira de tão arrepiado que me sentia. E não falo em específico, refiro-me sim ao geral do filme. 
Frases e diálogos extremamente bem conseguidos, com duplos significados e de compreensão profunda.
Mas o que mais me impressionou foi mesmo o rumo que deram: não é um filme sobre guerra, é um filme sobre jornalismo de guerra. Tudo o que sofrem, toda a adrenalina que sentem no momento… Simplesmente TUDO, bem representado por cada personagem apresentada.
Não minto que dava um final diferente à história, com toques mais satíricos e hiperbólicos, mas overall, muito bem conseguido.
Sobre esta crítica? Não consigo descrever melhor… “Yeah, that'll do.”

films.name: Ex Machina
films.review:
Well… after a long battle with "Men (2022)", my expectations for another film by Alex were pretty low, even though I'd been told this would be an interesting experience for me. As I started watching, I immediately noticed a familiar pattern already “implemented” in "Men (2022)" — the themes of misogyny and sexism. I honestly didn't expect that kind of message, that kind of moral approach, in a story like this.

And I have to be frank… aside from Oscar Isaac, the actors didn't really impress me, and I genuinely think they could've given a lot more.

It's a very solid film, but at the same time, I feel like it could've explored a lot more. Still, I'm giving it 4 stars… Let's just hope I don't regret that rating if I ever decide to rewatch it.

films.name: Warfare
films.review:
Some people are really trying to twist the purpose of this film – I feel disoriented just reading some of the reviews.
This is not a propaganda piece, nor is it trying to label anyone as a hero or a villain. It's meant to portray the cowardice and dehumanization seen throughout the Iraq War – both in the actions of Iraqi forces and in the cruelty of many American soldiers.
This is an anti-war film, with a remarkably simple plot but filled with thought-provoking elements worth discussing. I just found it unnecessary to show images of the soldiers at the end, some with blurred or covered faces.

A film with a high level of difficulty in execution that still delivers – both in cinematography and in how it conveys pain, aggression, and fear through all its actors.

Anthony Russo.name: Anthony Russo
Anthony Russo.bio:
Anthony Russo (born February 3, 1970) is an American filmmaker with his brother, Joe Russo, collectively known as the Russo brothers (/ˈruːsoʊ/ ROO-soh). They direct most of their work together. They are best known for directing four films in the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU): Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014), Captain America: Civil War(2016), Avengers: Infinity War (2018), and Avengers: Endgame (2019). Endgame grossed over $2.798 billion worldwide, briefly becoming the highest-grossing film ever. 

The brothers have also worked as directors and producers on the comedy series Arrested Development (2003–2005), Community (2009–2014), and Happy Endings (2011–2012). They won a Primetime Emmy Award for Arrested Development.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Russo brothers, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

Antoine Fuqua.name: Antoine Fuqua
Antoine Fuqua.bio: Antoine Fuqua (born May 30, 1965) is an American director. He began his career directing music videos for artists such as Toni Braxton, Coolio, Stevie Wonder, and Prince. His first feature film was the John Woo-produced action film The Replacement Killers (1998), starring Chow Yun Fat.  He then directed the crime thriller Training Day (2001), for which star Denzel Washington won an Academy Award for Best Actor. His next films were the action war drama Tears of the Sun (2003), the Arthurian legend film King Arthur (2004), the conspiracy action thriller Shooter (2007), the crime film Brooklyn's Finest (2009), and the action thrillers Olympus Has Fallen (2013) and The Equalizer (2014), the latter of which again paired Fuqua with Denzel Washington. He has also directed the biopics Emancipation (2022), about an escaped slave named Peter, and Michael (2026), about the popstar Michael Jackson.

films.name: Michael
films.review:
I have to thank Ms. Maria (the lady cleaning the theater during the credits) for the moment,  singing and dancing together in an already empty cinema: was genuinely so much fun.

I went to see this film alone. I wish I could’ve watched it with my mom, but unfortunately she had work the next day and it was impossible. She was the one who introduced me to Michael Jackson through my grandfather’s record player, with a tiny 45 RPM vinyl filled with his biggest hits: unfortunately, they couldn’t afford more at the time, so that’s what they had. I still remember telling my mom that I wanted to dance as well as Michael, and trying to do the moonwalk in front of her. It went badly. Very badly. She laughed at me, and honestly, I’m completely okay with that.

I didn’t walk into the cinema expecting the greatest film of all time. I didn’t expect the best CGI either (some moments looked decent, others somehow looked worse than every episode of She-Hulk). I went in hoping for a haunting performance (it was almost uncomfortable how accurate it felt), an overwhelming urge to stand up, sing, and dance, and sound design from another world.

That’s exactly what I got, and I’m happy with that.

I’m giving this rating because of everything this project represents to me, because of the experience I had (shout out to Ms. Maria, an incredible dancer with a broom), and because the casting was absolutely insane.

Ari Aster.name: Ari Aster
Ari Aster.bio:
Ari Aster (born July 15, 1986) is an American filmmaker. His films are noted for their unsettling combination of dark comedy, horror, and graphic violence. After garnering some recognition for writing and directing the short horror film The Strange Thing About the Johnsons (2011) as part of his studies at the AFI Conservatory, he became widely known for writing and directing the horror films Hereditary (2018) and Midsommar (2019), establishing himself in the genre of elevated horror.

Aster co-founded the production company Square Peg with Danish producer Lars Knudsen in 2019. He pivoted away from horror by writing, directing, and producing the surrealist comedy-drama film Beau Is Afraid (2023) and the satirical neo-Western thriller film Eddington (2025). Both films received positive reviews but failed to recoup their budgets at the box office.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Ari Aster, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.
Ari Aster.birthplace: New York City, New York, USA

films.name: Hereditary
films.review:
This film is the culmination of everything I want in a horror film. Probably one of the best scripts I've ever seen on a project of this kind. So many symbolisms, so many precedents for everything that will happen, everything so well planned. We see it from Annie's perspective but we get to understand much more than she does and that was simply GENIUS. 
Chilling music, jumpscares that left my heart in my mouth, simply horrific and chilling content, it's truly something "a la Ari Aster"... You're crazy, I sincerely hope you never change, because we need more things like this.

films.name: Eddington
films.review:
Watching it a second time made my rating change, and honestly, I wasn’t expecting that to happen at all. The final 40 minutes put any human being to the test - especially anyone aware of the sheer stupidity of what they’re witnessing. The fact that everything takes place in a single county, where the homeless man may be insane but the community can be even more so… it’s deeply satirical, and chilling to see a Sheriff portrayed this way. It’s almost unbelievable to experience this film from start to finish and take in the sheer number of extremes it represents.

Ari Aster, you already know everything I think about you and your films. But it never hurts to say it again: you’re one of my favorite directors, and that’s going to be very hard to change.

films.name: Midsommar
films.review:
Eu juro que estava indeciso entre 4 e 4 estrelas e meia. Passo a explicar: tinha e tenho medo de que as próximas vezes que vir este filme, não goste ainda mais do projeto. Tenho medo que esta masterpiece não cresça em mim de forma devida. 
Sim, disse masterpiece. Porque a realidade é que o Ari Aster foi capaz de transformar uma história "simples" em algo completamente macabro. Aquilo que mais me incomodava é que sempre conseguia perceber que alguma coisa se passava, mesmo no início do filme. Porque os atores e as próprias cenas foram tão minuciosamente colocadas que ficavas com a mera impressão de que algo iria acontecer. E acontecia, claro, é um filme de horror. Com poucos jumpscares (estava a contar), com simbolismos interessantíssimos, discursos perversos... E capaz de te mostrar o quão complicada pode ser a mente humana - complicada, bárbara, e por mais contraditório que seja, uma mente humana bastante desumana. 
Não dou 5 estrelas pelo que disse no início: quero voltar a ver isto, quero tentar perceber algumas cenas colocadas e acima de tudo quero entender a cabeça do Aster porque sinceramente... O gajo é um tolo varrido.

films.name: Munchausen
films.review:
This guy has family problems… Seriously. 
The soundtrack was a bit loud… But I’ll say it again, I really like you Aster🫶

films.name: Basically
films.review:
Pareceu mais um filme do Wes Anderson do que um do Ari Aster… 
Vou falar com o agente imobiliário do homem para ver se arranjo uma casa destas, sonho.

films.name: The Strange Thing About the Johnsons
films.review:
This is f****d up man. This is too much for me to handle and I love weird projects. Not THIS weird. Like damn, that man was horny. It's a solid short film, but again... Damn.

Aster, my friend, if you want to talk about your family, just do it...

Ps: The actor who played the father also appears as the protagonist in the first "Beau" and I have to say, once again, that I still don't like his performances very much. Sorry.

Barry Sonnenfeld.name: Barry Sonnenfeld
Barry Sonnenfeld.bio:
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia.

Barry Sonnenfeld (born April 1, 1953) is an American filmmaker and television director. He worked as cinematographer for the Coen Brothers, then later he directed and produced big budget films such as Men in Black.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Barry Sonnenfeld, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.​
Barry Sonnenfeld.birthplace: New York City, New York, USA

films.name: Men
films.review:
I don’t know what rating to give this because, while I enjoyed some scenes (visually stunning ones), others left me pretty angry — and a bit cringed out.  

The first thing I have to mention: a man making a movie about how awful men are is already a bit weird, at least in my books. What makes that feeling even worse is watching Alex double down on that message — because, yeah, that’s the whole moral of the story: men are garbage. Watching an hour and forty minutes just about that… well, I can’t even finish the sentence.  

One of the worst scenes — a shot that made me swallow hard and honestly stands as one of the worst I’ve seen in my limited cinema experience — was her eating the apple, tying into the Adam and Eve story. I won’t dive into that too much because I could spend hours writing about it, and it’s not worth it.  

Now, onto the positives — the ones that justify the rating you’ve already seen. The performances were outstanding, truly brilliant. The dynamic between Harper and James is incredibly well-explored (and deeply uncomfortable, it gave me chills). The choice to have the same actor play multiple roles and the use of the "Green Man" as symbolism kept me invested through to the end. The use of green was brilliantly explored, the house being painted red on the inside, the tilted cross behind the priest — these small details kept me at least somewhat invested in the story.

Benny Safdie.name: Benny Safdie
Benny Safdie.bio:
Benjamin Safdie (born February 24, 1986) is an American filmmaker, actor, and editor best known for his collaborations with his older brother, Josh Safdie. Together, the Safdie brothers have established themselves as leading voices in contemporary independent cinema with acclaimed films such as Heaven Knows What (2014), Good Time (2017), and Uncut Gems (2019).

In addition to directing, Benny has pursued a parallel career as an actor. His performance in Good Time earned him a nomination for the Independent Spirit Award for Best Supporting Male. He has since appeared in high-profile films including Paul Thomas Anderson’s Licorice Pizza (2021), Kelly Fremon Craig’s Are You There God? It’s Me, Margaret. (2023), and Christopher Nolan’s Oppenheimer (2023).

On television, he co-created, co-wrote, and starred alongside Nathan Fielder and Emma Stone in the Showtime series The Curse (2023).
Benny Safdie.birthplace: New York City, New York, USA

films.name: Good Time
films.review: "Beau is Afraid" but indie style, with less steroids, and in my opinion: much better.

films.name: Uncut Gems
films.review:
Is it strange to say that I had forgotten just how insane the final 40 minutes of this film are? Maybe. But at the same time, it feels natural, because I can’t remember any other film capable of making me feel this way - in such a dirty, relentless manner. Adam Sandler is so charismatic that you can’t help but root for his character until the very end, which makes everything more gripping and much harder to digest once it’s over.
Everything in this film is, in my view, executed extremely well, and the direction does almost all the magic - whether through the camerawork or the near-psychotic, stress-inducing editing. It’s a modern classic, and revisiting it only reinforces my stance on the film and further solidifies everything I have to say about this feature and about the Safdie brothers.

films.name: Goldman v Silverman
films.review:
I’m mentally preparing myself for two upcoming Safdie brothers films, because I really don’t want either of them to miss. “Uncut Gems” and “Good Time” are easily two of the best films of the 21st century — and honestly, they’ve proudly made it into my list of favorite A24 projects, and favorite films ever. Seeing the two brothers going their separate creative ways genuinely scares me. Protect them at all costs.

This short film captures perfectly the struggle of a street performer trying to showcase his art. A sharp critique of society, of the absurd “designated performance zones” in major cities (not just in New York — I see plenty of that here in Portugal, in Porto), and of the humiliation and mockery that exist even within the profession itself. Seven minutes that fly by naturally — simple, yet incredibly powerful.

films.name: Heaven Knows What
films.review:
Another film from the Safdie brothers — and believe me when I say I keep getting more and more amazed by the quality they showed early on in their international independent work.
A film with a “simple” premise managed to transport me into the darker side of New York, filled with social struggles and substance abuse — a project that exposes the city’s decay in an almost documentary-like way, portraying a painful path that’s incredibly hard to escape.

I’m also blown away by the performances and by the way they chose to tell the story. I honestly thought I’d find it boring, but that hour and a half just flew by.

Protect them. I’ve heard that "The Smashing Machine (2025)" isn’t selling well, and that really worries me. I just hope everything goes right for them.

films.name: Daddy Longlegs
films.review:
Still preparing to dive into their solo films.

The first serious project by the Safdie brothers manages to offer plenty of positives: a production that, no matter how simple it may seem, touches the soul — almost in a documentary-like format — and a story that maintains its sensibility, strength, and emotion from beginning to end.
I started watching the feature film without knowing it was inspired by the Safdie brothers’ real experiences with their father. But as I said, the production itself hinted that there was something genuine behind those scenes.
Deeply moving — at its core, it’s a portrait filled with both love and pain. The Safdies don’t paint the father as a villain, nor do they romanticize him. They show him as someone who loves his children deeply but is incapable of taking care of them in a stable way — a bittersweet blend of tenderness and irresponsibility.
Very solid for a first film, and an incredible tribute to their childhood.

films.name: The Smashing Machine
films.review:
🏆 THE 98TH OSCARS PREPARATIONS 🏆

Also in preparation for Marty Supreme. These guys never fail to surprise me. The Safdie brothers manage to make films with premises that aren’t really my thing, yet still completely hook me from start to finish the moment I hit the spacebar on my keyboard. This one is no exception.

And if I’ve seen people criticizing the film’s quality, I’m here to push back against that and say this: if there’s cinematography I absolutely LOVE, it’s the kind this film delivers. If there’s editing that gets me completely addicted, barely blinking, it’s this. Benny, my friend, you genuinely surprised me. I thought I was about to watch something fairly standard, but instead I ended up seeing a project with an image and a use of camera that left me speechless, especially for a film like this, where it makes perfect sense.

Great performances all around, fully had me engaged with the script from the very beginning. Emily Blunt is at her best here, genuinely chilling, and this might honestly be Dwayne Johnson’s strongest role to date.

No, it’s not a perfect film. I also don’t plan on revisiting it anytime soon, and that’s probably why I’m settling on 4 stars. But one thing I’ll keep doing is defending the Safdie brothers, because they’ve already proven they can make incredible films with or without each other. And when I say I won’t be rewatching it soon, that has more to do with the story than anything else - simply put, I’m not really a fan of wrestling, combat sports, or anything that revolves around people beating each other up.

films.name: Toback Vs. Mailer: The Incident
films.review:
Why did I watch this?

Picture this: I’m tired, I’ve spent the whole day studying and working on projects, it’s already late, and I’m not about to start a full-length film now… So I decided to stick with something short. I wanted to watch "Two Ships (2025)", but I couldn’t find it anywhere, so I went digging through the filmography of directors I love, and ended up finding this Safdie short.

Are there positives? Sure. You can very easily tell that James Toback and Norman Mailer are complete pieces of shit (trust me, it’s good to know that). At the same time, the way the café scenes blend with the animated segments was brilliant, and the animation itself is genuinely fun, well made, with a style that feels very different from anything I’ve seen before.

It killed some time, and honestly, I’m glad I didn’t end up on TikTok just to fall asleep, because at least I watched something… educational:

GAMBLING GAMBLING GAMBLING.
ALCOHOL ALCOHOL ALCOHOL.

Bo Burnham.name: Bo Burnham
Bo Burnham.bio:
Robert Pickering "Bo" Burnham (born August 21, 1990) is an American comedian, musician, actor, film director, screenwriter, and poet. He began his career on YouTube in March 2006, with his videos gaining more than 300 million views as of March 2021.

Burnham signed a four-year record deal with Comedy Central Records and released his debut EP, Bo fo Sho, in 2008. His first full-length album, Bo Burnham, was released the following year. At the age of 18, he became the youngest person to record a half-hour comedy special with Comedy Central. In 2010, his second album, Words Words Words, was released along with his first live comedy special of the same name on Comedy Central. His third album and second comedy special, what., was released in 2013 on his YouTube channel and Netflix. He finished in first place at the 2011 Comedy Central Stand-up Showdown. His third stand-up comedy special, Make Happy, was released exclusively on Netflix in 2016. His fourth comedy special, Bo Burnham: Inside, premiered on Netflix in 2021.

In 2013, Burnham co-created and starred in the MTV television series Zach Stone Is Gonna Be Famous and released a book of poetry called Egghead: Or, You Can't Survive on Ideas Alone. His first feature film as a writer and director, Eighth Grade, was released in 2018 to widespread critical acclaim; among other accolades, it received the Writers Guild of America Award for Best Original Screenplay and the Directors Guild of America Award for Outstanding Directing – First-Time Feature Film. In 2020, Burnham starred as Ryan Cooper in the film Promising Young Woman.

films.name: Bo Burnham: Inside
films.review:
This is very  special to me. In every possible way. A safe place, a different world or some cozy spot to not be in panic.

Hope you’re doing fine Bo, we miss you…

films.name: Bo Burnham: Make Happy
films.review:
It makes me cry every time I see it.
This project is so important to me…

films.name: Bo Burnham: What.
films.review:
Every time I hear the phrase "He isolated himself for 5 years in the pursuit of comedy", I run to Spotify and blast "Look Who's Inside Again". 

The neighbours must have been pissed off at me.

films.name: Bo Burnham: The Inside Outtakes
films.review:
“I DON’T KNOW WHAT’S HAPPENING
WHAT THE FUCK IS GOING IN?” - The definition of my life.

Still perfect.

films.name: Eighth Grade
films.review:
I can’t quite put into words what this movie made me feel, but I’ll try, I promise... I connected deeply with Kayla — the struggle of navigating puberty, especially when everything around you feels like a fog, uncertain and unexplored.  

The car scene left me completely uncomfortable, and I can’t explain the anger I felt watching it all unfold "right in front of me." The conversation between Kayla and her dad was truly special, showing not only her struggle with being a teenager but also his "unknowingness" in being — well, a dad.  

It was a moment where I genuinely felt understood. We’re all a bit strange in our own way, and I know I wasn’t easy to handle when I was younger. Bo, you never stop surprising me — you’re simply brilliant, and it’s a joy to watch everything you create. Thank you for showing such a complex and sometimes "dark" side of this age in such a powerful, honest way.

Bong Joon Ho.name: Bong Joon Ho
Bong Joon Ho.bio:
Bong Joon-ho (Korean: 봉준호, Korean pronunciation:[poːŋ tɕuːnho → poːŋdʑunɦo]; born September 14, 1969) is a South Korean filmmaker. The recipient of three Academy Awards, his work is characterised by emphasis on social and class themes, genre-mixing, dark comedy, and sudden tone shifts.

He first became known to audiences and achieved a cult following with his directorial debut film, the black comedy Barking Dogs Never Bite (2000), before achieving both critical and commercial success with his subsequent films: the crime thriller Memories of Murder (2003), the monster film The Host (2006), the science fiction action film Snowpiercer (2013), which served as Bong's English language debut, and the acclaimed black comedy thriller Parasite (2019), all of which are among the highest-grossing films in South Korea, with Parasite also being the highest-grossing South Korean film in history.

All of Bong's films have been South Korean productions. However, Snowpiercer, Okja (2017) and Mickey 17 (2025) are Hollywood co-productions with significant use of the English language. Two of his films have been screened in competitions at the Cannes Film Festival — Okja in 2017 and Parasite in 2019; the latter earned the Palme d'Or, the first for a South Korean film. Considered an immediate favourite by the Academy Awards, Parasite became the first South Korean film to receive Academy Award nominations, with Bong winning Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Original Screenplay, making Parasite the first film in the award's history not in English to win Best Picture. In 2017, Bong was included on Metacritic's list of the 25 best film directors of the 21st century. In 2020, Bong was included in Time's annual list of 100 Most Influential People and Bloomberg 50.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Bong Joon-ho, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

films.name: Memories of Murder
films.review:
I’m in complete shock.
Still thinking about the ending, still wondering if it was one person or another. Still processing the absurd number of suspects, all of them seemingly “normal.” I won’t go into the film’s specific message because I don’t want to spoil it — I don’t want to take away the pleasure of experiencing it for the first time, just as I did. Glued to the screen from the very first to the very last minute, ending with a final gasp and absolute shock. A masterpiece in every sense. A story that will stay with me forever.

films.name: Mickey 17
films.review:
This is the culmination of a sleepless night, an empty bottle of Vodka, a heated discussion about the state of the world and a comedy special starring myself, Diogo. 

What's more fun than this? Only my grade for the last subject I need to complete my degree.

Brett Ratner.name: Brett Ratner
Brett Ratner.bio:
Brett Ratner (born March 28, 1969) is an American film director and producer. He directed the Rush Hour film series, The Family Man, Red Dragon, X-Men: The Last Stand, Tower Heist, and Hercules. He is a producer of several films, including the Horrible Bosses series, as well as executive producer on other projects, including the films The Revenant and War Dogs and the television series Prison Break.

Ratner got his start directing with music videos in the 1990s and directed his first motion picture, Money Talks, in 1997. Overall, the films Ratner has directed have earned over $2 billion at the global box office. Ratner is the co-founder of RatPac Entertainment, a film production company. Ratner led RatPac's partnership with Dune Entertainment in September 2013 for a co-producing deal with Warner Bros. that included 75 films. RatPac Entertainment has co-financed 81 theatrically released motion pictures, exceeding $17 billion in worldwide box office receipts. RatPac's co-financed films have been nominated for 59 Academy Awards, 25 Golden Globes, and 43 BAFTAs and have won 25 Academy Awards, 8 Golden Globes, and 24 BAFTAs. In January 2017, Ratner received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame for his contributions to the motion picture industry, located at 6801 Hollywood Boulevard.

In 2017, numerous women in Hollywood came forward with allegations of sexual misconduct, sexual assault, or harassment against Ratner.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Brett Ratner, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

Bryan Singer.name: Bryan Singer
Bryan Singer.bio:
Bryan Singer (born September 17, 1965) is an American film director and producer. Singer won critical acclaim for his work on The Usual Suspects, and is especially well-known among fans of the science fiction and comic book genres for his work on the first two X-Men films and Superman Returns.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Bryan Singer, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.
Bryan Singer.birthplace: New York City, New York, USA

films.name: The Usual Suspects
films.review:
I won't lie: for a moment I thought it was one character, but in the end it turned out to be another… It ended up being somewhat predictable and yet completely unpredictable at the same time, I honestly can't explain this properly without spoiling certain scenes.

The truth is, it entertained me from start to finish, and the actors put on an absolute show when it comes to presenting their characters. We understand every action, we understand their mindset, even without knowing their pasts - not only because the story is incredibly well structured to allow that, but also thanks to the extraordinary performances.

I think the film works perfectly as a whole, and I genuinely feel like I'll revisit it with great pleasure.

The initial interrogation scene with the suspects is actually a blooper, but it works incredibly well, it feels so natural, so genuine. The way Benicio del Toro handled the moment made it even more special. What an amazing scene.

Chad Stahelski.name: Chad Stahelski
Chad Stahelski.bio:
Charles F. Stahelski (born September 20, 1968) is an American stuntman, stunt actor, and filmmaker. He is considered a highly influential figure in the action film genre. He first achieved prominence as a stunt performer and coordinator, notably as the key stunt double for Keanu Reeves on The Matrix (1999) and as the martial arts stunt coordinator on its first two sequels. He subsequently directed the 2014 film John Wick, starring Reeves, and its three sequels.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Chad Stahelski, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

Christopher Nolan.name: Christopher Nolan
Christopher Nolan.bio:
Sir Christopher Edward Nolan (born 30 July 1970) is a British and American filmmaker. As a significant auteur of his generation, he has established himself as a major figure of 21st-century Hollywood. His films have earned over $6 billion worldwide, making him the seventh highest-grossing film director of all time. Nolan's accolades include two Academy Awards, a Golden Globe Award and two British Academy Film Awards. He was appointed as a CBE in 2019 and was knighted in 2024 for his contributions to film.

Nolan developed an interest in filmmaking from a young age.
